titles.english: Chapter: There are four Takbir's in the funeral prayers

titles.arabic: باب قِرَاءَةِ فَاتِحَةِ الْكِتَابِ عَلَى الْجَنَازَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The recitation of Surah Al-Fathiha in the funeral Salat

titles.arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى الْقَبْرِ بَعْدَ مَا يُدْفَنُ
titles.english: Chapter: To offer the (funeral) Salat on the grave after the burial of the deceased

titles.arabic: باب الْمَيِّتُ يَسْمَعُ خَفْقَ النِّعَالِ
titles.english: Chapter: A dead person hears the footsteps (of the living)

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَحَبَّ الدَّفْنَ فِي الأَرْضِ الْمُقَدَّسَةِ أَوْ نَحْوِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever desired to be buried in the Sacred Land

titles.arabic: باب الدَّفْنِ بِاللَّيْلِ وَدُفِنَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ ـ رضى الله عنه ـ لَيْلاً
titles.english: Chapter: Burial at night

titles.arabic: باب بِنَاءِ الْمَسْجِدِ عَلَى الْقَبْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Building a mosque (a place of worship) at a grave

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ يَدْخُلُ قَبْرَ الْمَرْأَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Who may get down in the grave of a woman

titles.arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى الشَّهِيدِ
titles.english: Chapter: The funeral Salat (prayer) of a martyr

titles.arabic: باب دَفْنِ الرَّجُلَيْنِ وَالثَّلاَثَةِ فِي قَبْرٍ وَاحِدٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The burial of two or three men in one grave

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ غَسْلَ الشُّهَدَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever thinks no bath is required for the martyrs

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ يُقَدَّمُ فِي اللَّحْدِ
titles.english: Chapter: Who should be put first in the Lahd (a side extension of a grave)

titles.arabic: باب الإِذْخِرِ وَالْحَشِيشِ فِي الْقَبْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The placing of Idhkhir and grass in the grave

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يُخْرَجُ الْمَيِّتُ مِنَ الْقَبْرِ وَاللَّحْدِ لِعِلَّةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Can the dead body be taken out of its grave

titles.arabic: باب اللَّحْدِ وَالشَّقِّ فِي الْقَبْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Lahd (a side extension of a grave) and the (straight) cut in the grave

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا أَسْلَمَ الصَّبِيُّ فَمَاتَ هَلْ يُصَلَّى عَلَيْهِ وَهَلْ يُعْرَضُ عَلَى الصَّبِيِّ الإِسْلاَمُ
titles.english: Chapter: If a boy becomes a Muslim and then dies, should a funeral prayer be offered for him? Should Islam be explained to a boy?

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا قَالَ الْمُشْرِكُ عِنْدَ الْمَوْتِ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ
titles.english: Chapter: If Al-Mushrik says, "La ilaha illallah" at the time of his death

titles.arabic: باب الْجَرِيدِ عَلَى الْقَبْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Placing a leaf of a date palm over the grave

titles.arabic: باب مَوْعِظَةِ الْمُحَدِّثِ عِنْدَ الْقَبْرِ، وَقُعُودِ أَصْحَابِهِ حَوْلَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Preacher delivering a lecture at a grave and the sitting of his com anions around him

titles.arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي قَاتِلِ النَّفْسِ
titles.english: Chapter: What is said about committing suicide

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى الْمُنَافِقِينَ وَالاِسْتِغْفَارِ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ
titles.english: Chapter: It is disliked to offer the funeral prayer for the hypocrites, and to ask Allah's Forgiveness for the Mushrikun

titles.arabic: باب ثَنَاءِ النَّاسِ عَلَى الْمَيِّتِ
titles.english: Chapter: The praising of a deceased by the people

titles.arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The punishment in the grave

titles.arabic: باب التَّعَوُّذِ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: To seek refuge from the punishment in the grave

titles.arabic: باب عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ مِنَ الْغِيبَةِ وَالْبَوْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: Punishment in the grave because of back-biting and soiling one's clothes with urine

titles.arabic: باب الْمَيِّتِ يُعْرَضُ عَلَيْهِ بِالْغَدَاةِ وَالْعَشِيِّ
titles.english: Chapter: The deceased is shown his actual place (in Paradise or in Hell)

titles.arabic: باب كَلاَمِ الْمَيِّتِ عَلَى الْجَنَازَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The speech of the deceased upon the bier

titles.arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي أَوْلاَدِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
titles.english: Chapter: The dead children of Muslims

titles.arabic: باب مَا قِيلَ فِي أَوْلاَدِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
titles.english: Chapter: The (dead) children of Al-Mushrikun

titles.arabic: باب مَوْتِ يَوْمِ الاِثْنَيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Dying on Monday

titles.arabic: باب مَوْتِ الْفَجْأَةِ الْبَغْتَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Sudden unexpected death

titles.arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي قَبْرِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَأَبِي بَكْرٍ وَعُمَرَ ـ رضى الله عنهما
titles.english: Chapter: The graves of the Prophet (pbuh), Abu Bakr, and Umar Radiyallahu Anhu

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُنْهَى مِنْ سَبِّ الأَمْوَاتِ
titles.english: Chapter: What is forbidden as regards abusing the dead

titles.arabic: باب ذِكْرِ شِرَارِ الْمَوْتَى
titles.english: Chapter: Talking about the wicked among the dead

titles.arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الزَّكَاةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The obligation of Zakat

titles.arabic: باب الْبَيْعَةِ عَلَى إِيتَاءِ الزَّكَاةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To give Bai'a (pledge) for paying Zakat

titles.arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَانِعِ الزَّكَاةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The sin of a person who does not pay Zakat

titles.arabic: باب مَا أُدِّيَ زَكَاتُهُ فَلَيْسَ بِكَنْزٍ
titles.english: Chapter: A property from which the Zakat is paid is not Al-Kanz (hoarded-money)

titles.arabic: باب إِنْفَاقِ الْمَالِ فِي حَقِّهِ
titles.english: Chapter: To spend money in the right way

titles.arabic: بَابُ الرِّيَاءِ فِي الصَّدَقَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Giving alms for showing off

titles.arabic: بَابُ لاَ يَقْبَلُ اللَّهُ صَدَقَةً مِنْ غُلُولٍ وَلاَ يَقْبَلُ إِلاَّ مِنْ كَسْبٍ طَيِّبٍ
titles.english: Chapter: No charity is accepted from what is grabbed from the war booty in an illegal way

titles.arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةِ مِنْ كَسْبٍ طَيِّبٍ
titles.english: Chapter: As-Sadaqa must be from money earned honestly

titles.arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةِ قَبْلَ الرَّدِّ
titles.english: Chapter: To practise charity before such time when nobody would accept it

titles.arabic: باب اتَّقُوا النَّارَ وَلَوْ بِشِقِّ تَمْرَةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: "Protect yourself from Hell-fire even with half a date"

titles.arabic: باب فضل صدقة الشحيح الصحيح
titles.english: Chapter: What kind of As-Sadaqa (charity etc.) is superior?

titles.arabic: بَابُ صَدَقَةِ الْعَلاَنِيَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To give in charity openly

titles.arabic: بَابُ صَدَقَةِ السِّرِّ
titles.english: Chapter: To give in charity secretly

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا تَصَدَّقَ عَلَى غَنِيٍّ وَهُوَ لاَ يَعْلَمُ
titles.english: Chapter: If one gives an object of charity to a wealthy person unknowingly

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا تَصَدَّقَ عَلَى ابْنِهِ وَهُوَ لاَ يَشْعُرُ
titles.english: Chapter: If a person gives something in charity to his own son unknowingly

titles.arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةِ بِالْيَمِينِ
titles.english: Chapter: To give objects of charity with the right hand

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَمَرَ خَادِمَهُ بِالصَّدَقَةِ وَلَمْ يُنَاوِلْ بِنَفْسِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever ordered his servant to give something in charity and did not give it himself

titles.arabic: باب لاَ صَدَقَةَ إِلاَّ عَنْ ظَهْرِ غِنًى
titles.english: Chapter: The wealthy only are required to give in charity

titles.arabic: بَابُ الْمَنَّانِ بِمَا أَعْطَى
titles.english: Chapter: The one who follows up his charitable gifts with reminders of generosity

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَحَبَّ تَعْجِيلَ الصَّدَقَةِ مِنْ يَوْمِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever loves to give something in charity on the very day (he receives his earnings)

titles.arabic: باب التَّحْرِيضِ عَلَى الصَّدَقَةِ وَالشَّفَاعَةِ فِيهَا
titles.english: Chapter: To exhort one to give in charity

titles.arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةِ فِيمَا اسْتَطَاعَ
titles.english: Chapter: To give in charity as much as you can afford

titles.arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةُ تُكَفِّرُ الْخَطِيئَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: As-Sadaqa (charity) expiates sins

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ تَصَدَّقَ فِي الشِّرْكِ ثُمَّ أَسْلَمَ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever gave things in charity while he was a Mushrik and then embraced Islam

titles.arabic: باب أَجْرِ الْخَادِمِ إِذَا تَصَدَّقَ بِأَمْرِ صَاحِبِهِ غَيْرَ مُفْسِدٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The servant gets a reward for giving charity when ordered by the owner

titles.arabic: باب أَجْرِ الْمَرْأَةِ إِذَا تَصَدَّقَتْ أَوْ أَطْعَمَتْ مِنْ بَيْتِ زَوْجِهَا غَيْرَ مُفْسِدَةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The reward of the lady who gives in charity, from her husbandâ€™s house

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَى وَاتَّقَى وَصَدَّقَ بِالْحُسْنَى فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْيُسْرَى وَأَمَّا مَنْ بَخِلَ وَاسْتَغْنَى وَكَذَّبَ بِالْحُسْنَى فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْعُسْرَى}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Ta'ala: "As for the one who is charitable, mindful of Allah, and firmly believes in the finest reward, We will facilitate for them the Way of Ease. And as for the one who is stingy, indifferent to Allah, and staunchly denies the finest reward, We will facilitate for them the path of hardship.' (92:5-10)

titles.arabic: باب مَثَلِ الْمُتَصَدِّقِ وَالْبَخِيلِ
titles.english: Chapter: The examples of an alms-giver and a miser

titles.arabic: بَابُ صَدَقَةِ الْكَسْبِ وَالتِّجَارَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Giving in charity from the earnings and trade

titles.arabic: باب عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ صَدَقَةٌ، فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَلْيَعْمَلْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ
titles.english: Chapter: Every Muslim has to give in charity

titles.arabic: باب قَدْرُ كَمْ يُعْطَى مِنَ الزَّكَاةِ وَالصَّدَقَةِ وَمَنْ أَعْطَى شَاةً
titles.english: Chapter: How much is Zakat, and how much may be given in charity? And whoever gave a sheep in charity.

titles.arabic: باب زَكَاةِ الْوَرِقِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Zakat of silver

titles.arabic: باب الْعَرْضِ فِي الزَّكَاةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Zakat may be paid in kind

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يُجْمَعُ بَيْنَ مُتَفَرِّقٍ وَلاَ يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَ مُجْتَمِعٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The individual property of different people should neither be gathered together nor the joint property should be divided, in assessing the Zakat

titles.arabic: باب مَا كَانَ مِنْ خَلِيطَيْنِ فَإِنَّهُمَا يَتَرَاجَعَانِ بَيْنَهُمَا بِالسَّوِيَّةِ
titles.english: Chapter: If a property is equally owned by two partners, its Zakat is to be paid as a whole

titles.arabic: باب زَكَاةِ الإِبِلِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Zakat of camels

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ بَلَغَتْ عِنْدَهُ صَدَقَةُ بِنْتِ مَخَاضٍ وَلَيْسَتْ عِنْدَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever has to pay a one-year-old she-camel as Zakat and has not got it

titles.arabic: باب زَكَاةِ الْغَنَمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Zakat of sheep

titles.arabic: باب لاَ تُؤْخَذُ فِي الصَّدَقَةِ هَرِمَةٌ وَلاَ ذَاتُ عَوَارٍ وَلاَ تَيْسٌ إِلاَّ مَا شَاءَ الْمُصَدِّقُ
titles.english: Chapter: Neither an old, nor a defective animal, nor a male-goat may be taken as Zakat

titles.arabic: باب أَخْذِ الْعَنَاقِ فِي الصَّدَقَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To accept a young (female) goat as Zakat

titles.arabic: باب لاَ تُؤْخَذُ كَرَائِمُ أَمْوَالِ النَّاسِ فِي الصَّدَقَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: "Do not take the best from the property as Zakat"

titles.arabic: باب لَيْسَ فِيمَا دُونَ خَمْسِ ذَوْدٍ صَدَقَةٌ
titles.english: Chapter: There is no Zakat for less than five camels

titles.arabic: باب زَكَاةِ الْبَقَرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Zakat of cows

titles.arabic: باب الزَّكَاةِ عَلَى الأَقَارِبِ
titles.english: Chapter: The giving of Zakat to relatives

titles.arabic: باب لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِ فِي فَرَسِهِ صَدَقَةٌ
titles.english: Chapter: No Zakat on the horse of a Muslim

titles.arabic: باب لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِ فِي عَبْدِهِ صَدَقَةٌ
titles.english: Chapter: No Zakat on the slave belonging to a Muslim

titles.arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةِ عَلَى الْيَتَامَى
titles.english: Chapter: Giving in charity to orphans

titles.arabic: باب الزَّكَاةِ عَلَى الزَّوْجِ وَالأَيْتَامِ فِي الْحَجْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The giving of Zakat to husband and to orphans

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَفِي الرِّقَابِ}، {وَفِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Ta'ala: "(Alms-tax should be spent)... to free the slaves; and for those in debt; and for Allah's cause..." (9:60)

titles.arabic: باب الاِسْتِعْفَافِ عَنِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To abstain from begging

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَعْطَاهُ اللَّهُ شَيْئًا مِنْ غَيْرِ مَسْأَلَةٍ وَلاَ إِشْرَافِ نَفْسٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The one whom Allah gives something without his asking for it, or without avarice for it

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ سَأَلَ النَّاسَ تَكَثُّرًا
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever asks the people to increase his wealth

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {لاَ يَسْأَلُونَ النَّاسَ إِلْحَافًا} وَكَمِ الْغِنَى
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah 'Azza wa Jall: "... They do not beg people persistently. ..." (2:273) And who may be considered to have enough to be content and abstain from begging"

titles.arabic: باب خَرْصِ التَّمْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Estimating the date-fruits on the palms for Zakat

titles.arabic: باب الْعُشْرِ فِيمَا يُسْقَى مِنْ مَاءِ السَّمَاءِ وَبِالْمَاءِ الْجَارِي
titles.english: Chapter: One-twentieth on the yield of the land irrigated by rain or running water

titles.arabic: باب لَيْسَ فِيمَا دُونَ خَمْسَةِ أَوْسُقٍ صَدَقَةٌ
titles.english: Chapter: There is no Zakat on less than five Awsuq

titles.arabic: باب أَخْذِ صَدَقَةِ التَّمْرِ عِنْدَ صِرَامِ النَّخْلِ وَهَلْ يُتْرَكُ الصَّبِيُّ فَيَمَسُّ تَمْرَ الصَّدَقَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Zakat of dates should be taken during their plucking season. Can a child touch the dates collected as Zakat?

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ بَاعَ ثِمَارَهُ أَوْ نَخْلَهُ أَوْ أَرْضَهُ أَوْ زَرْعَهُ، وَقَدْ وَجَبَ فِيهِ الْعُشْرُ أَوِ الصَّدَقَةُ فَأَدَّى الزَّكَاةَ مِنْ غَيْرِهِ أَوْ بَاعَ ثِمَارَهُ وَلَمْ تَجِبْ فِيهِ الصَّدَقَةُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever sold his fruits, trees, land or crops and gave Zakat from some other property

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يَشْتَرِي صَدَقَتَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Can one buy the thing which he has given in charity?

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ فِي الصَّدَقَةِ لِلنَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
titles.english: Chapter: What is said regarding what is given to the Prophet (saws) and his offspring in charity

titles.arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةِ عَلَى مَوَالِي أَزْوَاجِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
titles.english: Chapter: As-Sadaqa for the freed slave-girls of the wives of the Prophet ﷺ

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا تَحَوَّلَتِ الصَّدَقَةُ
titles.english: Chapter: When alms is transferred

titles.arabic: باب أَخْذِ الصَّدَقَةِ مِنَ الأَغْنِيَاءِ وَتُرَدَّ فِي الْفُقَرَاءِ حَيْثُ كَانُوا
titles.english: Chapter: Zakat should be taken from the rich and given to the poor

titles.arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الإِمَامِ وَدُعَائِهِ لِصَاحِبِ الصَّدَقَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The invoking and supplicating of the Imam for the one who gives in charity

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُسْتَخْرَجُ مِنَ الْبَحْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: (Is Zakat imposed on) what is taken out of the sea

titles.arabic: باب فِي الرِّكَازِ الْخُمُسُ
titles.english: Chapter: There is Khumus on Rikaz

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَالْعَامِلِينَ عَلَيْهَا} وَمُحَاسَبَةِ الْمُصَدِّقِينَ مَعَ الإِمَامِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Most High: "... And those employed to collect (the funds) ..." (9:60) And the Imam is to supervise and check the work of the collectors.

titles.arabic: باب اسْتِعْمَالِ إِبِلِ الصَّدَقَةِ وَأَلْبَانِهَا لأَبْنَاءِ السَّبِيلِ
titles.english: Chapter: The use of the camels and their milk given as Zakat

titles.arabic: باب وَسْمِ الإِمَامِ إِبِلَ الصَّدَقَةِ بِيَدِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Branding the camels given in As-Sadaqa (Zakat) by the Imam with his own hands

titles.arabic: باب فَرْضِ صَدَقَةِ الْفِطْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Obligation of Sadaqat-ul-Fitr

titles.arabic: باب صَدَقَةِ الْفِطْرِ عَلَى الْعَبْدِ وَغَيْرِهِ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
titles.english: Chapter: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr on the free or slave Muslims

titles.arabic: باب صَاعٍ مِنْ شَعِيرٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is one Sa' of barley

titles.arabic: باب صَدَقَةِ الْفِطْرِ صَاعًا مِنْ طَعَامٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is one Sa' of meal (per head)

titles.arabic: باب صَدَقَةِ الْفِطْرِ صَاعًا مِنْ تَمْرٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is one Sa' of dates (per head)

titles.arabic: باب صَاعٍ مِنْ زَبِيبٍ
titles.english: Chapter: (Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is) one Sa' of raisins (dried grapes)

titles.arabic: باب الصَّدَقَةِ قَبْلَ الْعِيدِ
titles.english: Chapter: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is to be given before the 'Eid prayers

titles.arabic: باب صَدَقَةِ الْفِطْرِ عَلَى الْحُرِّ وَالْمَمْلُوكِ
titles.english: Chapter: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr on a slave as well as on a free Muslim

titles.arabic: باب صَدَقَةِ الْفِطْرِ عَلَى الصَّغِيرِ وَالْكَبِيرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Sadaqat-ul-Fitr is obligatory on the young and the old

titles.arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الْحَجِّ وَفَضْلِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: It is obligatory to perform Hajj (once in a life time) and its superiority

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {يَأْتُوكَ رِجَالاً وَعَلَى كُلِّ ضَامِرٍ يَأْتِينَ مِنْ كُلِّ فَجٍّ عَمِيقٍ لِيَشْهَدُوا مَنَافِعَ لَهُمْ}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Most High: "Call ˹all˺ people to the pilgrimage.1 They will come to you on foot and on every lean camel from every distant path, so they may obtain the benefits ˹in store˺ for them, ..."

titles.arabic: باب الْحَجِّ عَلَى الرَّحْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: To go for Hajj on a pack-saddle (of a camel)

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْحَجِّ الْمَبْرُورِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Al-Hajj-ul-Mabrur

titles.arabic: باب فَرْضِ مَوَاقِيتِ الْحَجِّ وَالْعُمْرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The demarcation of Mawaqit for Hajj

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَتَزَوَّدُوا فَإِنَّ خَيْرَ الزَّادِ التَّقْوَى}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Most High: "And take ˹necessary˺ provisions ˹for the journey˺—surely the best provision is righteousness." (2:197)

titles.arabic: باب مُهَلِّ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ لِلْحَجِّ وَالْعُمْرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Miqat of Hajj and 'Umra for the people of Makkah

titles.arabic: باب مِيقَاتِ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَلاَ يُهِلُّوا قَبْلَ ذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Miqat for the people of Al-Madina

titles.arabic: باب مُهَلِّ أَهْلِ الشَّأْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Miqat for the people of Sham

titles.arabic: باب مُهَلِّ أَهْلِ نَجْدٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The Miqat for the people of Najd

titles.arabic: باب مُهَلِّ مَنْ كَانَ دُونَ الْمَوَاقِيتِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Miqat for the people living within Mawaqit

titles.arabic: باب مُهَلِّ أَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Miqat for the people of Yemen

titles.arabic: باب ذَاتُ عِرْقٍ لأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Miqat for the people of â€˜Iraq is Dhat-â€˜Irq

titles.arabic: باب خُرُوجِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم عَلَى طَرِيقِ الشَّجَرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The going of the Prophet (saws) (for Hajj)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ الْعَقِيقُ وَادٍ مُبَارَكٌ ‏"‏‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: "Al-'Aqiq is a blessed valley."

titles.arabic: باب غَسْلِ الْخَلُوقِ ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ
titles.english: Chapter: To wash the perfume thrice of Ihram

titles.arabic: باب الطِّيبِ عِنْدَ الإِحْرَامِ
titles.english: Chapter: The use of perfume while assuming Ihram

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَهَلَّ مُلَبِّدًا
titles.english: Chapter: Talbiya and Ihram with head-hair matted (with resin or the like)

titles.arabic: باب الإِهْلاَلِ عِنْدَ مَسْجِدِ ذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To recite talbiya and assume Ihram at the mosque of Dhul-Hulaifa

titles.arabic: باب مَا لاَ يَلْبَسُ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ
titles.english: Chapter: What kind of clothes a Muhrim should not wear

titles.arabic: باب الرُّكُوبِ وَالاِرْتِدَافِ فِي الْحَجِّ
titles.english: Chapter: Riding alone or with somebody else during Hajj

titles.arabic: باب مَا يَلْبَسُ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَالأَرْدِيَةِ وَالأُزُرِ
titles.english: Chapter: What kind of clothes a Muhrim should wear

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ بَاتَ بِذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ حَتَّى أَصْبَحَ
titles.english: Chapter: Passing the night in Dhul-Hulaifa till dawn

titles.arabic: باب رَفْعِ الصَّوْتِ بِالإِهْلاَلِ
titles.english: Chapter: Talbiya is to be recited aloud

titles.arabic: باب التَّلْبِيَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Talbiya

titles.arabic: باب التَّحْمِيدِ وَالتَّسْبِيحِ وَالتَّكْبِيرِ قَبْلَ الإِهْلاَلِ عِنْدَ الرُّكُوبِ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The praising and glorification of Allah and saying Takbir before Talbiya, while mounting animal

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَهَلَّ حِينَ اسْتَوَتْ بِهِ رَاحِلَتُهُ قائِمَة
titles.english: Chapter: Reciting Talbiya when one has mounted his mount and it stood up straight (ready to set out)

titles.arabic: باب الإِهْلاَلِ مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبْلَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Reciting Talbiya while facing the Qiblah

titles.arabic: باب التَّلْبِيَةِ إِذَا انْحَدَرَ فِي الْوَادِي
titles.english: Chapter: Reciting Talbiya on entering a valley

titles.arabic: باب كَيْفَ تُهِلُّ الْحَائِضُ وَالنُّفَسَاءُ
titles.english: Chapter: How should a menstruating woman and a woman in a puerperal state assume Ihram?

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَهَلَّ فِي زَمَنِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم كَإِهْلاَلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever assumed Ihram with the same intention as that of the Prophet (saws)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {الْحَجُّ أَشْهُرٌ مَعْلُومَاتٌ فَمَنْ فَرَضَ فِيهِنَّ الْحَجَّ فَلاَ رَفَثَ وَلاَ فُسُوقَ وَلاَ جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ}
titles.english: Chapter: "The Hajj is (in) the well-known (lunar year) months ..."

titles.arabic: باب التَّمَتُّعِ وَالإِقْرَانِ وَالإِفْرَادِ بِالْحَجِّ وَفَسْخِ الْحَجِّ لِمَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ مَعَهُ هَدْى
titles.english: Chapter: Hajj-at-Tamattu', Hajj-al-Qiran, and Hajj-al-Ifrad

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَبَّى بِالْحَجِّ وَسَمَّاهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Talbiya for Hajj and the intention of Hajj

titles.arabic: باب التَّمَتُّعِ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ
titles.english: Chapter: Hajj-at-Tamattu'

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {ذَلِكَ لِمَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ أَهْلُهُ حَاضِرِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ}
titles.english: Chapter: "This is for him whose family is not present at the Al-Masjid-al-Haram"

titles.arabic: باب الاِغْتِسَالِ عِنْدَ دُخُولِ مَكَّةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Taking a bath on entering Makkah

titles.arabic: باب دُخُولِ مَكَّةَ نَهَارًا أَوْ لَيْلاً
titles.english: Chapter: To enter Makkah by day or by night

titles.arabic: باب مِنْ أَيْنَ يَدْخُلُ مَكَّةَ
titles.english: Chapter: From where to enter Makkah

titles.arabic: باب مِنْ أَيْنَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ مَكَّةَ
titles.english: Chapter: From where to leave Makkah

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَكَّةَ وَبُنْيَانِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Makkah and its buildings, and the statement of Allah Ta'ala

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْحَرَمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of the Haram (of Makkah)

titles.arabic: باب تَوْرِيثِ دُورِ مَكَّةَ وَبَيْعِهَا وَشِرَائِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The inheritance, sale and purchase of the houses of Makkah

titles.arabic: باب نُزُولِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مَكَّةَ
titles.english: Chapter: The residence of the Prophet (saws) in Makkah

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَذَا الْبَلَدَ آمِنًا وَاجْنُبْنِي وَبَنِيَّ أَنْ نَعْبُدَ الأَصْنَامَ رَبِّ إِنَّهُنَّ أَضْلَلْنَ كَثِيرًا مِنَ النَّاسِ فَمَنْ تَبِعَنِي فَإِنَّهُ مِنِّي وَمَنْ عَصَانِي فَإِنَّكَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ رَبَّنَا إِنِّي أَسْكَنْتُ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي بِوَادٍ غَيْرِ ذِي زَرْعٍ عِنْدَ بَيْتِكَ الْمُحَرَّمِ رَبَّنَا لِيُقِيمُوا الصَّلاَةَ فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ} الآيَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Most High: "And ˹remember˺ when Abraham prayed, “My Lord! Make this city ˹of Mecca˺ secure, and keep me and my children away from the worship of idols. My Lord! They have caused many people to go astray. So whoever follows me is with me, and whoever disobeys me—then surely You are ˹still˺ All-Forgiving, Most Merciful. Our Lord! I have settled some of my offspring in a barren valley, near Your Sacred House, our Lord, so that they may establish prayer. So make the hearts of ˹believing˺ people incline towards them ..." (14:35-37)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {جَعَلَ اللَّهُ الْكَعْبَةَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ قِيَامًا لِلنَّاسِ وَالشَّهْرَ الْحَرَامَ وَالْهَدْيَ وَالْقَلاَئِدَ ذَلِكَ لِتَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الأَرْضِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Most High: "Allah has made the Ka’bah—the Sacred House—a sanctuary of well-being for all people, along with the sacred months, the sacrificial animals, and the ˹offerings decorated with˺ garlands. All this so you may know that Allah knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and that He has ˹perfect˺ knowledge of everything."

titles.arabic: باب كِسْوَةِ الْكَعْبَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The covering of the Ka'bah

titles.arabic: باب هَدْمِ الْكَعْبَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The demolishing of the Ka'bah

titles.arabic: باب مَا ذُكِرَ فِي الْحَجَرِ الأَسْوَدِ
titles.english: Chapter: What is said regarding the Black Stone

titles.arabic: باب إِغْلاَقِ الْبَيْتِ وَيُصَلِّي فِي أَىِّ نَوَاحِي الْبَيْتِ شَاءَ
titles.english: Chapter: Closing the door of the Ka'bah and Salat in it

titles.arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي الْكَعْبَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Offering As-Salat (the prayers) inside the Ka'bah

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَدْخُلِ الْكَعْبَةَ وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا يَحُجُّ كَثِيرًا وَلاَ يَدْخُلُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever did not enter the Ka'bah, and Ibn 'Umar رضي الله عنهما used to perform Hajj frequently without entering the Ka'bah

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ كَبَّرَ فِي نَوَاحِي الْكَعْبَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Saying Takbir inside the Ka'bah

titles.arabic: باب كَيْفَ كَانَ بَدْءُ الرَّمَلِ
titles.english: Chapter: How (the legality of) the Ramal started

titles.arabic: باب اسْتِلاَمِ الْحَجَرِ الأَسْوَدِ حِينَ يَقْدَمُ مَكَّةَ أَوَّلَ مَا يَطُوفُ وَيَرْمُلُ ثَلاَثًا
titles.english: Chapter: The touching (and kissing) of the Black Stone and Ramal

titles.arabic: باب الرَّمَلِ فِي الْحَجِّ وَالْعُمْرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Doing Ramal in Tawaf during Hajj and 'Umra

titles.arabic: باب اسْتِلاَمِ الرُّكْنِ بِالْمِحْجَنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Touching the Black Stone with a stick

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَلِمْ إِلاَّ الرُّكْنَيْنِ الْيَمَانِيَيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The two Yemenite Corners of the Ka'bah

titles.arabic: باب تَقْبِيلِ الْحَجَرِ
titles.english: Chapter: To kiss the Black Stone

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَشَارَ إِلَى الرُّكْنِ إِذَا أَتَى عَلَيْهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever pointed towards the Corner (Black Stone)

titles.arabic: باب التَّكْبِيرِ عِنْدَ الرُّكْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: To say Takbir in front of the Corner (having the Black Stone)

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ إِذَا قَدِمَ مَكَّةَ، قَبْلَ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ إِلَى بَيْتِهِ، ثُمَّ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ إِلَى الصَّفَا
titles.english: Chapter: Tawaf of the Ka'bah on reaching Makkah

titles.arabic: باب طَوَافِ النِّسَاءِ مَعَ الرِّجَالِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Tawaf of women and men

titles.arabic: باب الْكَلاَمِ فِي الطَّوَافِ
titles.english: Chapter: The permissibility of talking during the Tawaf

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا رَأَى سَيْرًا أَوْ شَيْئًا يُكْرَهُ فِي الطَّوَافِ قَطَعَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever saw a string during the Tawaf and cut it

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ عُرْيَانٌ وَلاَ يَحُجُّ مُشْرِكٌ
titles.english: Chapter: It is neither permissible for a naked person to perform Tawaf nor Hajj for a Mushrik

titles.arabic: بَابُ إِذَا وَقَفَ فِي الطَّوَافِ
titles.english: Chapter: If one stops during the Tawaf (should he start from the beginning?)

titles.arabic: باب صَلَّى النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم لِسُبُوعِهِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Prophet ﷺ offered a two rak'ã prayer after his seven rounds (of Tawaf of the Ka'bah)

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَقْرَبِ الْكَعْبَةَ، وَلَمْ يَطُفْ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ إِلَى عَرَفَةَ، وَيَرْجِعَ بَعْدَ الطَّوَافِ الأَوَّلِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever did not go near the Ka'bah and did not perform Tawaf of the Ka'bah after the first Tawaf performed on entering Makkah till he proceeded to 'Arafat and returned

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَىِ الطَّوَافِ خَارِجًا مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ
titles.english: Chapter: Two Rak'a prayer of Tawaf outside the mosque

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَىِ الطَّوَافِ خَلْفَ الْمَقَامِ
titles.english: Chapter: Two Rak'a (prayer) of Tawaf behind Maqam-Ibrahim

titles.arabic: باب الطَّوَافِ بَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ وَالْعَصْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Tawaf after the morning and 'Asr prayer

titles.arabic: باب الْمَرِيضِ يَطُوفُ رَاكِبًا
titles.english: Chapter: A sick person may perform Tawaf while riding

titles.arabic: باب سِقَايَةِ الْحَاجِّ
titles.english: Chapter: Providing the pilgrims with water to drink

titles.arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي زَمْزَمَ
titles.english: Chapter: What is said about Zamzam (water)

titles.arabic: باب طَوَافِ الْقَارِنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Tawaf of Al-Qarin

titles.arabic: باب الطَّوَافِ عَلَى وُضُوءٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Tawaf with ablution

titles.arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ وَجُعِلَ مِنْ شَعَائِرِ اللَّهِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Tawaf (Sa'i) between As-Safa and Al-Marwa

titles.arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي السَّعْىِ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Sa'i between As-Safa and Al-Marwa

titles.arabic: باب تَقْضِي الْحَائِضُ الْمَنَاسِكَ كُلَّهَا إِلاَّ الطَّوَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ ، وَإِذَا سَعَى عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: A menstruating woman can perform all the ceremonies of Hajj except Tawaf

titles.arabic: بَابُ الإِهْلاَلِ مِنَ الْبَطْحَاءِ، وَغَيْرِهَا لِلْمَكِّيِّ وَلِلْحَاجِّ إِذَا خَرَجَ إِلَى مِنًى
titles.english: Chapter: Assuming Ihram from Al-Batha' and other places

titles.arabic: باب أَيْنَ يُصَلِّي الظُّهْرَ يَوْمَ التَّرْوِيَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Where to offer the Zuhr prayer on the day of Tarwiya

titles.arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ بِمِنًى
titles.english: Chapter: As-Salat at Mina

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ يَوْمِ عَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Fasting on the Day of 'Arafa (at 'Arafat)

titles.arabic: باب التَّلْبِيَةِ وَالتَّكْبِيرِ إِذَا غَدَا مِنْ مِنًى إِلَى عَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Talbiya and Takbir while proceeding from Mina to 'Arafat

titles.arabic: باب التَّهْجِيرِ بِالرَّوَاحِ يَوْمَ عَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: To proceed at noon on the Day of 'Arafa

titles.arabic: باب الْوُقُوفِ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ بِعَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Staying on one's riding animal at 'Arafat

titles.arabic: باب الْجَمْعِ بَيْنَ الصَّلاَتَيْنِ بِعَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: To offer the two Salat together at 'Arafat

titles.arabic: باب قَصْرِ الْخُطْبَةِ بِعَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: To shorten the Khutbah the Day of 'Arafat

titles.arabic: بَابُ التَّعْجِيلِ إِلَى الْمَوْقِفِ
titles.english: Chapter: To hurry up for the stay (at 'Arafat)

titles.arabic: باب الْوُقُوفِ بِعَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: The staying at 'Arafat

titles.arabic: باب السَّيْرِ إِذَا دَفَعَ مِنْ عَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Oneâ€™s speed while one is departing from 'Arafat

titles.arabic: باب النُّزُولِ بَيْنَ عَرَفَةَ وَجَمْعٍ
titles.english: Chapter: To dismount between 'Arafat and Jam'

titles.arabic: باب أَمْرِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِالسَّكِينَةِ عِنْدَ الإِفَاضَةِ، وَإِشَارَتِهِ إِلَيْهِمْ بِالسَّوْطِ
titles.english: Chapter: The order of the Prophet ﷺ that people should be calm and patient on proceeding (from 'Arafat) and the waving of his lash towards them

titles.arabic: باب الْجَمْعِ بَيْنَ الصَّلاَتَيْنِ بِالْمُزْدَلِفَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The offering of two Salat together at Al-Muzdalifa

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ جَمَعَ بَيْنَهُمَا وَلَمْ يَتَطَوَّعْ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever combined the two prayer at one time

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَذَّنَ وَأَقَامَ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدَةٍ مِنْهُمَا
titles.english: Chapter: Adhan and Iqama for each of them

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ قَدَّمَ ضَعَفَةَ أَهْلِهِ بِلَيْلٍ، فَيَقِفُونَ بِالْمُزْدَلِفَةِ وَيَدْعُونَ وَيُقَدِّمُ إِذَا غَابَ الْقَمَرُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whosoever sent the weak amongst his family (women and children) early (from Al-Muzdalifa to Mina) at night after the moon had set. They stayed at Al Muzdalifa and invoked Allah there and proceeded from there when the moon had set

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ يُصَلِّي الْفَجْرَ بِجَمْعٍ
titles.english: Chapter: At what time is the Fajr prayer to be offered at Jam'

titles.arabic: باب مَتَى يُدْفَعُ مِنْ جَمْعٍ
titles.english: Chapter: When to depart from Jam' (i.e., Al-Muzdalifa)

titles.arabic: باب التَّلْبِيَةِ وَالتَّكْبِيرِ غَدَاةَ النَّحْرِ ، حِينَ يَرْمِي الْجَمْرَةَ، وَالاِرْتِدَافِ فِي السَّيْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The recitation of Talbiya and Takbir in the morning of the day of Nahr till the Ramy of Jamarat-al-'Aqaba and riding behind somebody else on a riding animal

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {فَمَنْ تَمَتَّعَ بِالْعُمْرَةِ إِلَى الْحَجِّ فَمَا اسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ ثَلاَثَةِ أَيَّامٍ فِي الْحَجِّ وَسَبْعَةٍ إِذَا رَجَعْتُمْ تِلْكَ عَشَرَةٌ كَامِلَةٌ ذَلِكَ لِمَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ أَهْلُهُ حَاضِرِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ}
titles.english: Chapter: “...And whosoever performs the ‘Umra in the months of Hajj before (performing) the Hajj (i.e. Hajj-al-Qiran and Hajj-at-Tamattu‘) he must slaughter an animal (Hady) such as he can afford it... till... present at the Al-Masjid-al-Haram (at Makkah)...” (V.2:196)

titles.arabic: باب رُكُوبِ الْبُدْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The riding over the Budn

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ سَاقَ الْبُدْنَ مَعَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever drove the Budn alongwith him

titles.arabic: باب مَنِ اشْتَرَى الْهَدْىَ مِنَ الطَّرِيقِ
titles.english: Chapter: Buying the Hadi on the way

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَشْعَرَ وَقَلَّدَ بِذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ ثُمَّ أَحْرَمَ
titles.english: Chapter: Marking and garlanding (the Hadi) at Dhul-Hulaifa and then assuming Ihram

titles.arabic: باب فَتْلِ الْقَلاَئِدِ لِلْبُدْنِ وَالْبَقَرِ
titles.english: Chapter: To twist (and make) the garlands for the Budn

titles.arabic: باب إِشْعَارِ الْبُدْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The marking of the Budn (camels for sacrifice)

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ قَلَّدَ الْقَلاَئِدَ بِيَدِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: The garlands round the necks of the Hady

titles.arabic: باب تَقْلِيدِ الْغَنَمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The garlanding of sheep

titles.arabic: باب الْقَلاَئِدِ مِنَ الْعِهْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The garlands made from coloured wool

titles.arabic: باب تَقْلِيدِ النَّعْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: Garlanding (the Hadi) with a shoe

titles.arabic: باب الْجِلاَلِ لِلْبُدْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The covering (sheet) of the Budn

titles.arabic: باب مَنِ اشْتَرَى هَدْيَهُ مِنَ الطَّرِيقِ وَقَلَّدَهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The purchase of Hadi on the way and garlanding it

titles.arabic: باب ذَبْحِ الرَّجُلِ الْبَقَرَ عَنْ نِسَائِهِ، مِنْ غَيْرِ أَمْرِهِنَّ
titles.english: Chapter: To slaughter on behalf of oneâ€™s wives

titles.arabic: باب النَّحْرِ فِي مَنْحَرِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِمِنًى
titles.english: Chapter: To slaughter at the Manhar at Mina

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ نَحَرَ بِيَدِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Nahr (Slaughtering) with oneâ€™s own hands

titles.arabic: باب نَحْرِ الإِبِلِ مُقَيَّدَةً
titles.english: Chapter: Slaughtering the camels after tying their one leg

titles.arabic: باب نَحْرِ الْبُدْنِ قَائِمَةً
titles.english: Chapter: To slaughter the camels while they are standing

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يُعْطَى الْجَزَّارُ مِنَ الْهَدْىِ شَيْئًا
titles.english: Chapter: The butcher should not be given anything of the Hadi

titles.arabic: باب يُتَصَدَّقُ بِجُلُودِ الْهَدْىِ
titles.english: Chapter: The skins of Al-Hadi are to be given in charity

titles.arabic: باب يُتَصَدَّقُ بِجِلاَلِ الْبُدْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The covering sheets of Budn are to be given in charity

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَإِذْ بَوَّأْنَا لإِبْرَاهِيمَ مَكَانَ الْبَيْتِ أَنْ لاَ تُشْرِكْ بِي شَيْئًا وَطَهِّرْ بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْقَائِمِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ وَأَذِّنْ فِي النَّاسِ بِالْحَجِّ يَأْتُوكَ رِجَالاً وَعَلَى كُلِّ ضَامِرٍ يَأْتِينَ مِنْ كُلِّ فَجٍّ عَمِيقٍ لِيَشْهَدُوا مَنَافِعَ لَهُمْ وَيَذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ فِي أَيَّامٍ مَعْلُومَاتٍ عَلَى مَا رَزَقَهُمْ مِنْ بَهِيمَةِ الأَنْعَامِ فَكُلُوا مِنْهَا وَأَطْعِمُوا الْبَائِسَ الْفَقِيرَ ثُمَّ لْيَقْضُوا تَفَثَهُمْ وَلْيُوفُوا نُذُورَهُمْ وَلْيَطَّوَّفُوا بِالْبَيْتِ الْعَتِيقِ ذَلِكَ وَمَنْ يُعَظِّمْ حُرُمَاتِ اللَّهِ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَهُ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِ}
titles.english: Chapter: "And (remember) when We showed Ibrahim (Abraham) the site of the (Sacred) House, (Ka'bah at Makkah), (saying): 'Associate not anything (in worship) with Me [La ilaha illallah (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah) – Islamic Monotheism] and sanctify My House for those who circumambulate it, and those who stand up for prayer and those who bow (submit themselves with humility and obedience to Allah), and make prostration (in prayer).' “And proclaim to mankind the Hajj (pilgrimage). They will come to you on foot... up to... then that is better for him with his Lord.” (V.22:26-30)

titles.arabic: باب مَا يَأْكُلُ مِنَ الْبُدْنِ وَمَا يُتَصَدَّقُ
titles.english: Chapter: What is to be eaten of Budn and what to be distributed

titles.arabic: باب الذَّبْحِ قَبْلَ الْحَلْقِ
titles.english: Chapter: Slaughtering before having one's head shaved

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَبَّدَ رَأْسَهُ عِنْدَ الإِحْرَامِ وَحَلَقَ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever matted his head-hair on assuming Ihram

titles.arabic: باب الْحَلْقِ وَالتَّقْصِيرِ عِنْدَ الإِحْلاَلِ
titles.english: Chapter: To shave the head and to have the head-hair cut short on finishing the Ihram

titles.arabic: باب تَقْصِيرِ الْمُتَمَتِّعِ بَعْدَ الْعُمْرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To get the head-hair cut short after 'Umra

titles.arabic: باب الزِّيَارَةِ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The visit (of the Ka'bah) to perform (Tawaf-al-Ifada) on the Day of Nahr

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا رَمَى بَعْدَ مَا أَمْسَى أَوْ حَلَقَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَذْبَحَ نَاسِيًا أَوْ جَاهِلاً
titles.english: Chapter: Rami of the Jamra after Maghrib or head shaved before slaughtering the Hadi

titles.arabic: باب الْفُتْيَا عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ عِنْدَ الْجَمْرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To give religious verdicts while riding an animal

titles.arabic: باب الْخُطْبَةِ أَيَّامَ مِنًى
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Khutba during the Days of Mina

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يَبِيتُ أَصْحَابُ السِّقَايَةِ أَوْ غَيْرُهُمْ بِمَكَّةَ لَيَالِيَ مِنًى
titles.english: Chapter: May those who provide the pilgrims with water stay at Makkah during the nights of Mina?

titles.arabic: باب رَمْىِ الْجِمَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: To do the Rami of the Jimar

titles.arabic: باب رَمْىِ الْجِمَارِ مِنْ بَطْنِ الْوَادِي
titles.english: Chapter: Rami of Jimar from the middle of the valley

titles.arabic: باب رَمْىِ الْجِمَارِ بِسَبْعِ حَصَيَاتٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The Rami of the Jimar with seven small stones

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ رَمَى جَمْرَةَ الْعَقَبَةِ فَجَعَلَ الْبَيْتَ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Keeping the House (Ka'bah) the left on doing Rami of the Jamrat-ul-'Aqaba

titles.arabic: باب يُكَبِّرُ مَعَ كُلِّ حَصَاةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: To say 'Allahu Akbar' on throwing every pebble

titles.arabic: بَابُ مَنْ رَمَى جَمْرَةَ الْعَقَبَةِ وَلَمْ يَقِفْ
titles.english: Chapter: Not standing (for invocation) after doing Rami of Jamrat-ul-'Aqaba

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا رَمَى الْجَمْرَتَيْنِ يَقُومُ وَيُسْهِلُ مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبْلَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: After doing Rami of the two Jamrat one should invoke Allah

titles.arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْيَدَيْنِ عِنْدَ جَمْرَةِ الدُّنْيَا وَالْوُسْطَى
titles.english: Chapter: Invocation near Al-Jamrat-ud-Dunya and Al-Jamrat-ul-Wusta

titles.arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ عِنْدَ الْجَمْرَتَيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Invoking (Allah) near the two Jamrat

titles.arabic: باب الطِّيبِ بَعْدَ رَمْىِ الْجِمَارِ وَالْحَلْقِ قَبْلَ الإِفَاضَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To perfume after Rami and to have head shaved before Tawaf-al-Ifada

titles.arabic: باب طَوَافِ الْوَدَاعِ
titles.english: Chapter: Tawaf-al-Wada'

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا حَاضَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ بَعْدَ مَا أَفَاضَتْ
titles.english: Chapter: If a woman gets her menses after Tawaf-al-Ifada

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى الْعَصْرَ يَوْمَ النَّفْرِ بِالأَبْطَحِ
titles.english: Chapter: 'Asr prayer at Abtah on the day of departure from Mina (Day of Nafr)

titles.arabic: باب الْمُحَصَّبِ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Muhassab

titles.arabic: باب النُّزُولِ بِذِي طُوًى قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ مَكَّةَ، وَالنُّزُولِ بِالْبَطْحَاءِ الَّتِي بِذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ إِذَا رَجَعَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ
titles.english: Chapter: To camp at Dhi-Tuwa before entering Makkah and at Al-Bathaâ€™ on returning from Makkah

titles.arabic: بَابُ مَنْ نَزَلَ بِذِي طُوًى إِذَا رَجَعَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Staying at Dhi-Tuwa on returning from Makkah

titles.arabic: باب التِّجَارَةِ أَيَّامَ الْمَوْسِمِ وَالْبَيْعِ فِي أَسْوَاقِ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Trading during the time of Hajj

titles.arabic: باب الإِدْلاَجِ مِنَ الْمُحَصَّبِ
titles.english: Chapter: To depart from Al-Muhassab in the night

titles.arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الْعُمْرَةِ وَفَضْلِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: 'Umra and its superiority

titles.arabic: باب مَنِ اعْتَمَرَ قَبْلَ الْحَجِّ
titles.english: Chapter: 'Umra before Hajj

titles.arabic: باب كَمِ اعْتَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
titles.english: Chapter: How many times did the Prophet (saws) perform 'Umra ?

titles.arabic: باب عُمْرَةٍ فِي رَمَضَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: 'Umra in (the month of) Ramadan

titles.arabic: باب الْعُمْرَةِ لَيْلَةَ الْحَصْبَةِ وَغَيْرِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: 'Umra on the night of departure from Mina after Hajj and on other nights

titles.arabic: باب عُمْرَةِ التَّنْعِيمِ
titles.english: Chapter: 'Umra from At-Tan'im

titles.arabic: باب الاِعْتِمَارِ بَعْدَ الْحَجِّ بِغَيْرِ هَدْىٍ
titles.english: Chapter: 'Umra after performing Hajj without having a Hady

titles.arabic: باب أَجْرِ الْعُمْرَةِ عَلَى قَدْرِ النَّصَبِ
titles.english: Chapter: The reward of 'Umra is according to hardship

titles.arabic: باب الْمُعْتَمِرِ إِذَا طَافَ طَوَافَ الْعُمْرَةِ، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ، هَلْ يُجْزِئُهُ مِنْ طَوَافِ الْوَدَاعِ
titles.english: Chapter: If a person departs after performing the Tawaf of 'Umra, will that Tawaf substitute for Tawaf-al-Wada'?

titles.arabic: باب يَفْعَلُ فِي الْعُمْرَةِ مَا يَفْعَلُ فِي الْحَجِّ
titles.english: Chapter: The same ceremonies in 'Umra, as in Hajj

titles.arabic: باب مَتَى يَحِلُّ الْمُعْتَمِرُ
titles.english: Chapter: When a person performing 'Umra finish his Ihram?

titles.arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ إِذَا رَجَعَ مِنَ الْحَجِّ أَوِ الْعُمْرَةِ أَوِ الْغَزْوِ
titles.english: Chapter: What to say on returning from Hajj, 'Umra, Ghazwa

titles.arabic: باب اسْتِقْبَالِ الْحَاجِّ الْقَادِمِينَ وَالثَّلاَثَةِ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Reception of the returning pilgrims

titles.arabic: باب الْقُدُومِ بِالْغَدَاةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Arriving in the morning

titles.arabic: باب الدُّخُولِ بِالْعَشِيِّ
titles.english: Chapter: Returning after midday till sunset

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَطْرُقُ أَهْلَهُ إِذَا بَلَغَ الْمَدِينَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Not to go to family on arrival at night

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَسْرَعَ نَاقَتَهُ إِذَا بَلَغَ الْمَدِينَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: To proceed faster on reaching his town

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوَابِهَا}
titles.english: Chapter: "... So enter houses through their proper doors"

titles.arabic: باب السَّفَرُ قِطْعَةٌ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ
titles.english: Chapter: Travelling is a kind of torture

titles.arabic: باب الْمُسَافِرِ إِذَا جَدَّ بِهِ السَّيْرُ يُعَجِّلُ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: What to do to arrive home early?

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا أُحْصِرَ الْمُعْتَمِرُ
titles.english: Chapter: If one is prevented from 'Umra

titles.arabic: باب الإِحْصَارِ فِي الْحَجِّ
titles.english: Chapter: One who is prevented from performing the Hajj

titles.arabic: باب النَّحْرِ قَبْلَ الْحَلْقِ فِي الْحَصْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The slaughtering before shaving the head (in case) one is prevented

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمُحْصَرِ بَدَلٌ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever said that the Muhsar is not supposed to perform 'Umra or Hajj in lieu of the prevented one

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {فَمَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ مَرِيضًا أَوْ بِهِ أَذًى مِنْ رَأْسِهِ فَفِدْيَةٌ مِنْ صِيَامٍ أَوْ صَدَقَةٍ أَوْ نُسُكٍ}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah تعالى: "...And whosoever of you is ill or has an ailment in his scalp, (necessitating shaving), he must pay a Fidya (ransom), of either observing Saum (fasts) (three days), or giving Sadaqa (charity - feeding six poor persons), or offer sacrifice (one sheep)..." (V.2:196)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {أَوْ صَدَقَةٍ}
titles.english: Chapter: The saying of Allah Ta'ala "... Or giving Sadaqa ..." (V.2:196)

titles.arabic: باب الإِطْعَامُ فِي الْفِدْيَةِ نِصْفُ صَاعٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The Fidya (compensation for a missed or wrongly practised religious ceremony) in the form of feeding (six persons) each with one-half a Sa (of food)

titles.arabic: باب النُّسُكُ شَاةٌ
titles.english: Chapter: The Nusuk (offering) is one sheep

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى ‏ {‏فَلاَ رَفَثَ‏} ‏
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah تعالى: "...Then he should not have sexual relations (with his wife) ..." (V.2:197)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: {وَلاَ فُسُوقَ وَلاَ جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah عز وجل "...nor commit sin nor dispute unjustly during Hajj..." (V.2:197)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {لاَ تَقْتُلُوا الصَّيْدَ وَأَنْتُمْ حُرُمٌ وَمَنْ قَتَلَهُ مِنْكُمْ مُتَعَمِّدًا فَجَزَاءٌ مِثْلُ مَا قَتَلَ مِنَ النَّعَمِ يَحْكُمُ بِهِ ذَوَا عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ هَدْيًا بَالِغَ الْكَعْبَةِ أَوْ كَفَّارَةٌ طَعَامُ مَسَاكِينَ أَوْ عَدْلُ ذَلِكَ صِيَامًا لِيَذُوقَ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِ عَفَا اللَّهُ عَمَّا سَلَفَ وَمَنْ عَادَ فَيَنْتَقِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْهُ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انْتِقَامٍ أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُ مَتَاعًا لَكُمْ وَلِلسَّيَّارَةِ وَحُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَرِّ مَا دُمْتُمْ حُرُمًا وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ}
titles.english: Chapter: The penalty for hunting (by a Muhrim). And the Statement of Allah تعالى: "...Kill not game while you are in the state of Ihram for Hajj or Umra (pilgrimage), and whosoever of you kills it intentionally, the penalty is an offering, brought to the Ka'bah, of an eatable animal (i.e., sheep, goat, cow, etc.) brought to the Ka'bah, equivalent to the one he killed... to the end of the Verse... And fear Allah to Whom you shall be gathered back." (V.5:95-96).

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا صَادَ الْحَلاَلُ فَأَهْدَى لِلْمُحْرِمِ الصَّيْدَ أَكَلَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: If a non-Muhrim hunts (an animal) and gives it as a present to a Muhrim, (it is permissible for) the latter to eat it

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا رَأَى الْمُحْرِمُونَ صَيْدًا فَضَحِكُوا فَفَطِنَ الْحَلاَلُ
titles.english: Chapter: If the Muhrimun saw game and laughed and a non-Muhrim understood, they are allowed to eat the game

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يُعِينُ الْمُحْرِمُ الْحَلاَلَ فِي قَتْلِ الصَّيْدِ
titles.english: Chapter: A Muhrim should not help a non-Muhrim in the hunting of a game

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يُشِيرُ الْمُحْرِمُ إِلَى الصَّيْدِ لِكَىْ يَصْطَادَهُ الْحَلاَلُ
titles.english: Chapter: A Muhrim should not point at a game

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا أَهْدَى لِلْمُحْرِمِ حِمَارًا وَحْشِيًّا حَيًّا لَمْ يَقْبَلْ
titles.english: Chapter: If a person gave onager to a Muhrim then he should not accept it

titles.arabic: باب مَا يَقْتُلُ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الدَّوَابِّ
titles.english: Chapter: (What kind of) animals can be killed by a Muhrim

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يُعْضَدُ شَجَرُ الْحَرَمِ
titles.english: Chapter: It is not permissible to cut the trees of the Haram

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يُنَفَّرُ صَيْدُ الْحَرَمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The game in the Haram should not be chased

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَحِلُّ الْقِتَالُ بِمَكَّةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Fighting is prohibited in Makkah

titles.arabic: باب الْحِجَامَةِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Cupping for a Muhrim

titles.arabic: باب تَزْوِيجِ الْمُحْرِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The marrying of Muhrim

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُنْهَى مِنَ الطِّيبِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ وَالْمُحْرِمَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: What is forbidden for a Muhrim as regards perfumes

titles.arabic: باب الاِغْتِسَالِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The taking of a bath by a Muhrim

titles.arabic: باب لُبْسِ الْخُفَّيْنِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدِ النَّعْلَيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Wearing of Khuff by a Muhrim if slippers are not available

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدِ الإِزَارَ فَلْيَلْبَسِ السَّرَاوِيلَ
titles.english: Chapter: If an Izar is not available, one can wear trousers

titles.arabic: باب لُبْسِ السِّلاَحِ لِلْمُحْرِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Carrying of arms by a Muhrim

titles.arabic: باب دُخُولِ الْحَرَمِ وَمَكَّةَ بِغَيْرِ إِحْرَامٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Entering the Haram and Makkah without Ihram

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا أَحْرَمَ جَاهِلاً وَعَلَيْهِ قَمِيصٌ
titles.english: Chapter: If somebody ignorantly assumed Ihram while wearing a shirt (will Fidya be compulsory?)

titles.arabic: باب الْمُحْرِمِ يَمُوتُ بِعَرَفَةَ وَلَمْ يَأْمُرِ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ يُؤَدَّى عَنْهُ بَقِيَّةُ الْحَجِّ
titles.english: Chapter: A Muhrim died at 'Arafat and the Prophet (saws) did not order anybody to finish the remaining ceremonies of Hajj on his behalf

titles.arabic: باب سُنَّةِ الْمُحْرِمِ إِذَا مَاتَ
titles.english: Chapter: The legal way of (burying) a dead Muhrim

titles.arabic: باب الْحَجِّ وَالنُّذُورِ عَنِ الْمَيِّتِ وَالرَّجُلُ يَحُجُّ عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ‏
titles.english: Chapter: To perform Hajj on behalf of a dead person. A man can perform Hajj on behalf of a woman

titles.arabic: باب الْحَجِّ عَمَّنْ لاَ يَسْتَطِيعُ الثُّبُوتَ عَلَى الرَّاحِلَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Performing Hajj for a person who cannot sit firmly on the mount

titles.arabic: باب حَجِّ الْمَرْأَةِ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ
titles.english: Chapter: Performing Hajj by a woman on behalf of a man

titles.arabic: باب حَجِّ الصِّبْيَانِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Hajj of boys (children etc.)

titles.arabic: باب حَجِّ النِّسَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: Hajj of women

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ نَذَرَ الْمَشْىَ إِلَى الْكَعْبَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever vowed to go on foot to the Ka'bah

titles.arabic: باب حَرَمِ الْمَدِينَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Haram (sanctuary) of Al-Madina

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ، وَأَنَّهَا تَنْفِي النَّاسَ
titles.english: Chapter: Superiority of Al-Madina. It expels (evil) persons

titles.arabic: باب الْمَدِينَةُ طَابَةُ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Madina is also called Taba

titles.arabic: باب لاَبَتَىِ الْمَدِينَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The two mountains of Al-Madina

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ رَغِبَ عَنِ الْمَدِينَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The one who avoids living in Al-Madina?

titles.arabic: باب الإِيمَانُ يَأْرِزُ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Iman (Belief) returns and goes back to Al-Madina

titles.arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ كَادَ أَهْلَ الْمَدِينَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Sin of that person who harms the people of Al-Madina

titles.arabic: باب آطَامِ الْمَدِينَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The high buildings of Al-Madina

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَدْخُلُ الدَّجَّالُ الْمَدِينَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Ad-Dajjal will not be able to enter Al-Madina

titles.arabic: باب الْمَدِينَةُ تَنْفِي الْخَبَثَ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Madina expels all the evil and bad persons

titles.arabic: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم أَنْ تُعْرَى الْمَدِينَةُ
titles.english: Chapter: The dislike of the Prophet (saws) that Al-Madina should be vacated

titles.arabic: بَابُ فَضْلِ الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of knowledge

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ سُئِلَ عِلْمًا وَهُوَ مُشْتَغِلٌ فِي حَدِيثِهِ فَأَتَمَّ الْحَدِيثَ ثُمَّ أَجَابَ السَّائِلَ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever is asked about knowledge while he is busy in some conversation, so he finished talking and then answered the questioner

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ رَفَعَ صَوْتَهُ بِالْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever raises his voice in (conveying) knowledge

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ الْمُحَدِّثِ حَدَّثَنَا أَوْ، أَخْبَرَنَا وَأَنْبَأَنَا
titles.english: Chapter: Concerning the variety of words used by narrators conveying different significations regarding the concept of narrating and which has importance for the Hadith scholars only

titles.arabic: باب طَرْحِ الإِمَامِ الْمَسْأَلَةَ عَلَى أَصْحَابِهِ لِيَخْتَبِرَ مَا عِنْدَهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Imam questioning his companions in order to test their knowledge

titles.arabic: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: What is said about knowledge

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ فِي الْمُنَاوَلَةِ وَكِتَابِ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ بِالْعِلْمِ إِلَى الْبُلْدَانِ
titles.english: Chapter: What is said regarding the hand to hand exchange (of books of knowledge), and the writing of knowledge by religious scholars to different countries

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ قَعَدَ حَيْثُ يَنْتَهِي بِهِ الْمَجْلِسُ وَمَنْ رَأَى فُرْجَةً فِي الْحَلْقَةِ فَجَلَسَ فِيهَا‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever sat at the farther end of a gathering. And whoever found a place amongst a gathering and took his seat there

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏رُبَّ مُبَلَّغٍ أَوْعَى مِنْ سَامِعٍ ‏"‏
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of the Prophet (saws): lt is probable that a person who receives a piece of information indirectly may comprehend it better than he who has heard it directly from its source

titles.arabic: بَابُ الْعِلْمُ قَبْلَ الْقَوْلِ وَالْعَمَلِ
titles.english: Chapter: It is essential to know a thing first before saying or acting upon it.

titles.arabic: باب مَا كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يَتَخَوَّلُهُمْ بِالْمَوْعِظَةِ وَالْعِلْمِ كَىْ لاَ يَنْفِرُوا
titles.english: Chapter: The Prophet (saws) used to take care of the people in preaching by selecting a suitable time so that they might not run away (never made them averse or bored them with religious talk and knowledge all the time)

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ جَعَلَ لأَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ أَيَّامًا مَعْلُومَةً
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever fixed a special day for giving (a religious talk) to the students

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ يُرِدِ اللَّهُ بِهِ خَيْرًا يُفَقِّهْهُ فِي الدِّينِ
titles.english: Chapter: If Allah Jalla Jalalahu wants to do good to a person, He makes him comprehend (the religion). [The understanding of the Qur'an and As-Sunna (legal ways) of the Prophet (Muhammad (saws))]

titles.arabic: باب الْفَهْمِ فِي الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: (The superiority of) comprehending knowledge

titles.arabic: باب الاِغْتِبَاطِ فِي الْعِلْمِ وَالْحِكْمَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Wish to be like the one who has knowledge and Al-Hikmah [wisdom i.e., the knowledge of the Qur'an and the Sunna (legal ways) of the Prophet (saws)]

titles.arabic: باب مَا ذُكِرَ فِي ذَهَابِ مُوسَى صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي الْبَحْرِ إِلَى الْخَضِرِ
titles.english: Chapter: What has been said about the journey of Prophet Musa ('alaihi as-salam) (when he went) in the sea to meet Al-Khidr

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ اللَّهُمَّ عَلِّمْهُ الْكِتَابَ ‏"‏
titles.english: Chapter: The statement of the Prophet (saws): "O Allah! Bestow on him (Ibn Abbas) the knowledge of the Book (the Qur'an)

titles.arabic: باب مَتَى يَصِحُّ سَمَاعُ الصَّغِيرِ
titles.english: Chapter: At what age may a youth be listened to (i.e. quotation of the Hadith from a boy be acceptable)

titles.arabic: باب الْخُرُوجِ فِي طَلَبِ الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: To go out in search of knowledge

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ عَلِمَ وَعَلَّمَ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of a person who learns (Islam, becomes a religious scholar) and then teaches it to others

titles.arabic: باب رَفْعِ الْعِلْمِ وَظُهُورِ الْجَهْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: (What is said regarding) the disappearance of the (religious) knowledge and the appearance of (religious) ignorance

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of (religious) knowledge

titles.arabic: باب الْفُتْيَا وَهُوَ وَاقِفٌ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ وَغَيْرِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: To give a religious verdict while riding an animal or standing on anything else

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَجَابَ الْفُتْيَا بِإِشَارَةِ الْيَدِ وَالرَّأْسِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever gave a religious verdict by beckoning or by nodding

titles.arabic: بَابُ تَحْرِيضِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَفْدَ عَبْدِ الْقَيْسِ عَلَى أَنْ يَحْفَظُوا الإِيمَانَ وَالْعِلْمَ وَيُخْبِرُوا مَنْ وَرَاءَهُمْ
titles.english: Chapter: The Prophet (saws) urged the people (mission) of 'Abdul Qais to memorize the faith and the (religious) knowledge (as he explained to them) and to inform (convey) to their people whom they left behind (at home)

titles.arabic: باب الرِّحْلَةِ فِي الْمَسْأَلَةِ النَّازِلَةِ وَتَعْلِيمِ أَهْلِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: To travel seeking an answer to a problematic matter, and to teach it to one's family

titles.arabic: باب التَّنَاوُبِ فِي الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: To fix the duties in rotation for learning (religious) knowledge

titles.arabic: باب الْغَضَبِ فِي الْمَوْعِظَةِ وَالتَّعْلِيمِ إِذَا رَأَى مَا يَكْرَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: To be furious while preaching or teaching if one sees what one hates

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ بَرَكَ عَلَى رُكْبَتَيْهِ عِنْدَ الإِمَامِ أَوِ الْمُحَدِّثِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever knelt down before the Imam or a (religious) preacher

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَعَادَ الْحَدِيثَ ثَلاَثًا لِيُفْهَمَ عَنْهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Repeating ones talk thrice in order to make others understand

titles.arabic: باب تَعْلِيمِ الرَّجُلِ أَمَتَهُ وَأَهْلَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: A man teaching (religion to) his woman-slave and his family

titles.arabic: باب عِظَةِ الإِمَامِ النِّسَاءَ وَتَعْلِيمِهِنَّ
titles.english: Chapter: The preaching (and teaching) of the (religious) knowledge to women by the Imam (Chief)

titles.arabic: باب الْحِرْصِ عَلَى الْحَدِيثِ
titles.english: Chapter: Eagerness to (learn) the Hadith

titles.arabic: باب كَيْفَ يُقْبَضُ الْعِلْمُ
titles.english: Chapter: How the (religious) knowledge will be taken away

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يُجْعَلُ لِلنِّسَاءِ يَوْمٌ عَلَى حِدَةٍ فِي الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Should a day be fixed for women in order to teach them religion (apart from men)?

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ سَمِعَ شَيْئًا، فَرَاجَعَ حَتَّى يَعْرِفَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever heard something (but did not understand it) and then asked again till he understood it completely

titles.arabic: باب لِيُبَلِّغِ الْعِلْمَ الشَّاهِدُ الْغَائِبَ
titles.english: Chapter: It is incumbent on those who are present [in a religious meeting (or conference)] to convey the knowledge to those who are absent

titles.arabic: باب إِثْمِ مَنْ كَذَبَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
titles.english: Chapter: The sin of a person who tells a lie against the Prophet (saws)

titles.arabic: باب كِتَابَةِ الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The writing of knowledge

titles.arabic: باب الْعِلْمِ وَالْعِظَةِ بِاللَّيْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: The knowledge and its teaching and preaching at night

titles.arabic: باب السَّمَرِ بِالْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: To speak about (religious) knowledge at night

titles.arabic: باب حِفْظِ الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: (What is said regarding) the memorization of (religious) knowledge

titles.arabic: باب الإِنْصَاتِ لِلْعُلَمَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: To be quiet (and listen) to religious learned men

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ لِلْعَالِمِ إِذَا سُئِلَ أَىُّ النَّاسِ أَعْلَمُ فَيَكِلُ الْعِلْمَ إِلَى اللَّهِ
titles.english: Chapter: When a religious learned man is asked, "Who is the most learned person." it is better for him to attribute or entrust absolute knowledge to Allah 'Azza wa Jall and to say. "Allah is the Most Learned (than anybody else)"

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ سَأَلَ وَهْوَ قَائِمٌ عَالِمًا جَالِسًا
titles.english: Chapter: Whosoever, while standing, asked a religious learned man who was sitting (on a pulpit or a similar thing, about something)

titles.arabic: باب السُّؤَالِ وَالْفُتْيَا عِنْدَ رَمْىِ الْجِمَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: To ask about a religious matter and to give a religious verdict (at Mina during Hajj) while doing the Rami of Jimar (throwing of pebbles at the Jimar in Mina during Hajj)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَمَا أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلاَّ قَلِيلاً}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Ta'ala: "And of knowledge you (mankind) have been given only a little"

titles.arabic: بَابُ مَنْ تَرَكَ بَعْضَ الاِخْتِيَارِ مَخَافَةَ أَنْ يَقْصُرَ فَهْمُ بَعْضِ النَّاسِ عَنْهُ فَيَقَعُوا فِي أَشَدَّ مِنْهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whosoever left some optional things simply for the fear that some people may not be able to understand them and may fall into something more difficult

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ خَصَّ بِالْعِلْمِ قَوْمًا دُونَ قَوْمٍ كَرَاهِيَةَ أَنْ لاَ يَفْهَمُوا
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever selected some people to teach them (religious) knowledge preferring them over others for fear that the others may not understand it

titles.arabic: باب الْحَيَاءِ فِي الْعِلْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: (What is said as regards): To be shy (Al-Haya) while learning (religious) knowledge

titles.arabic: باب مَنِ اسْتَحْيَا فَأَمَرَ غَيْرَهُ بِالسُّؤَالِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whosoever felt shy (to ask something) and then requested another person to ask on his behalf

titles.arabic: باب ذِكْرِ الْعِلْمِ وَالْفُتْيَا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ
titles.english: Chapter: Teaching religious knowledge and giving religious verdicts in a masjid

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَجَابَ السَّائِلَ بِأَكْثَرَ مِمَّا سَأَلَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whosoever answered the questioner more than what was asked

titles.arabic: باب وُجُوبِ صَوْمِ رَمَضَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: Fasting is obligatory in (the month of) Ramadan

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ الصَّوْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of As-Saum (the fasting)

titles.arabic: باب الصَّوْمُ كَفَّارَةٌ
titles.english: Chapter: As-Saum (the fasting) is an expiation (for sins)

titles.arabic: باب الرَّيَّانُ لِلصَّائِمِينَ
titles.english: Chapter: Ar-Raiyan is for people observing Saum

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يُقَالُ رَمَضَانُ أَوْ شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ وَمَنْ رَأَى كُلَّهُ وَاسِعًا
titles.english: Chapter: Should it be said "Ramadan" or "the month of Ramadan"? And whoever thinks that both are permissible

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ صَامَ رَمَضَانَ إِيمَانًا وَاحْتِسَابًا وَنِيَّةً
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever observed fast in Ramadan out of sincere Faith with honest intention

titles.arabic: باب أَجْوَدُ مَا كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يَكُونُ فِي رَمَضَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: The Prophet saws used to be most generous in the month of Ramadan

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَدَعْ قَوْلَ الزُّورِ وَالْعَمَلَ بِهِ فِي الصَّوْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever does not give up lying speech while observing Saum

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يَقُولُ إِنِّي صَائِمٌ إِذَا شُتِمَ
titles.english: Chapter: Should one say, "I am observing Saum (fast)" on being abused?

titles.arabic: باب الصَّوْمِ لِمَنْ خَافَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ الْعُزْبَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: As-Saum for those who fear committing illegal sexual acts

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «إِذَا رَأَيْتُمُ الْهِلاَلَ فَصُومُوا وَإِذَا رَأَيْتُمُوهُ فَأَفْطِرُوا»
titles.english: Chapter: "Start observing Saum on seeing the crescent of Ramadan, and stop on seeing the crescent (of Shawwal)."

titles.arabic: باب شَهْرَا عِيدٍ لاَ يَنْقُصَانِ
titles.english: Chapter: The two months of Eid do not decrease

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ لاَ نَكْتُبُ وَلاَ نَحْسُبُ ‏"‏‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: "We neither write nor know accounts."

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَتَقَدَّمَنَّ رَمَضَانَ بِصَوْمِ يَوْمٍ وَلاَ يَوْمَيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Not to observe Saum for a day or two ahead of Ramadan

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ: {أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ لَيْلَةَ الصِّيَامِ الرَّفَثُ إِلَى نِسَائِكُمْ هُنَّ لِبَاسٌ لَكُمْ وَأَنْتُمْ لِبَاسٌ لَهُنَّ عَلِمَ اللَّهُ أَنَّكُمْ كُنْتُمْ تَخْتَانُونَ أَنْفُسَكُمْ فَتَابَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَعَفَا عَنْكُمْ فَالآنَ بَاشِرُوهُنَّ وَابْتَغُوا مَا كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah عز وجل: "It is made lawful for you to have sexual relations with your wives on the night of the As-Saum (the fasts). They are Libas, for you, and you are the same for them. Allah knows that you used to deceive yourselves, so He turned to you (accepted your repentance) and forgave you. So, now have sexual relations with them and seek that which Allah has ordained for you (offspring)…” (V.2:187)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَكُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا حَتَّى يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمُ الْخَيْطُ الأَبْيَضُ مِنَ الْخَيْطِ الأَسْوَدِ مِنَ الْفَجْرِ ثُمَّ أَتِمُّوا الصِّيَامَ إِلَى اللَّيْلِ}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah Taa'la: "And eat and drink until the white thread of dawn appears to you distinct from the black."

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ لاَ يَمْنَعَنَّكُمْ مِنْ سَحُورِكُمْ أَذَانُ بِلاَلٍ ‏"‏
titles.english: Chapter: "The Adhan of Bilal should not stop you from taking Sahur"

titles.arabic: باب تَأْخِيرِ السَّحُورِ
titles.english: Chapter: Taking the Sahur hurriedly (shortly before dawn)

titles.arabic: باب قَدْرِ كَمْ بَيْنَ السَّحُورِ وَصَلاَةِ الْفَجْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Interval between the Sahur and Salat-ul-Fajr

titles.arabic: باب بَرَكَةِ السَّحُورِ مِنْ غَيْرِ إِيجَابٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Sahur is a blessing but it is not compulsory

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا نَوَى بِالنَّهَارِ صَوْمًا
titles.english: Chapter: If the intention of Saum was made in the daytime.

titles.arabic: باب الصَّائِمِ يُصْبِحُ جُنُبًا
titles.english: Chapter: If a person observing Saum gets up in the morning in the state of Janaba

titles.arabic: باب الْمُبَاشَرَةِ لِلصَّائِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: To embrace while one is observing Saum (fast)

titles.arabic: باب الْقُبْلَةِ لِلصَّائِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Kissing by a fasting person

titles.arabic: باب اغْتِسَالِ الصَّائِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Taking a bath by a person observing Saum (fast)

titles.arabic: باب الصَّائِمِ إِذَا أَكَلَ أَوْ شَرِبَ نَاسِيًا
titles.english: Chapter: If a person observing Saum ate or drank forgetfully

titles.arabic: باب سِوَاكِ الرَّطْبِ وَالْيَابِسِ لِلصَّائِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Siwak for the person observing Saum (fast)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ فَلْيَسْتَنْشِقْ بِمَنْخِرِهِ الْمَاءَ». وَلَمْ يُمَيِّزْ بَيْنَ الصَّائِمِ وَغَيْرِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: "Whoever performs ablution should put water in his nose and then blow it out."

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا جَامَعَ فِي رَمَضَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: Sexual intercourse with wife in Ramadan.

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا جَامَعَ فِي رَمَضَانَ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ شَىْءٌ فَتُصُدِّقَ عَلَيْهِ فَلْيُكَفِّرْ
titles.english: Chapter: Sexual intercourse with wife in Ramadan and the expiation of that.

titles.arabic: باب الْمُجَامِعِ فِي رَمَضَانَ هَلْ يُطْعِمُ أَهْلَهُ مِنَ الْكَفَّارَةِ إِذَا كَانُوا مَحَاوِيجَ
titles.english: Chapter: To feed family from sin expiation (kaffarah) if they are needy.

titles.arabic: باب الْحِجَامَةِ وَالْقَىْءِ لِلصَّائِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: Cupping and vomiting in Saum (fast)

titles.arabic: باب الصَّوْمِ فِي السَّفَرِ وَالإِفْطَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: To observe Saum (fast) or not during journeys

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا صَامَ أَيَّامًا مِنْ رَمَضَانَ ثُمَّ سَافَرَ
titles.english: Chapter: If a person observed Saum for some days and then went on a journey.

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِمَنْ ظُلِّلَ عَلَيْهِ، وَاشْتَدَّ الْحَرُّ: «لَيْسَ مِنَ الْبِرِّ الصَّوْمُ فِي السَّفَرِ»
titles.english: Chapter: It is not righteousness to observe Saum on a journey

titles.arabic: باب لَمْ يَعِبْ أَصْحَابُ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا فِي الصَّوْمِ وَالإِفْطَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: Not to criticize each other for observing Saum or not (on journeys)

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَفْطَرَ فِي السَّفَرِ لِيَرَاهُ النَّاسُ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever broke his Saum (fast) on a journey (publicly).

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ يُطِيقُونَهُ فِدْيَةٌ طَعَامُ مِسْكِينٍ}
titles.english: Chapter: (The Statement of Allah تعالى): “And as for those who can fast with difficulty (e.g. the aged etc.) they have (a choice either to fast or) to feed a Miskin (poor person) (for every day).” (V.2:184)

titles.arabic: باب مَتَى يُقْضَى قَضَاءُ رَمَضَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: To make up for the missed days of fasting

titles.arabic: باب الْحَائِضِ تَتْرُكُ الصَّوْمَ وَالصَّلاَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: The menstruating should leave the Saum and Salat

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ مَاتَ وَعَلَيْهِ صَوْمٌ
titles.english: Chapter: Can somebody else observe Saum instead of the dead?

titles.arabic: باب مَتَى يَحِلُّ فِطْرُ الصَّائِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: When to break the Saum (fast)?

titles.arabic: باب يُفْطِرُ بِمَا تَيَسَّرَ عَلَيْهِ بِالْمَاءِ وَغَيْرِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Iftar (to break the Saum (fast))

titles.arabic: باب تَعْجِيلِ الإِفْطَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: To hasten the Iftar

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا أَفْطَرَ فِي رَمَضَانَ ثُمَّ طَلَعَتِ الشَّمْسُ
titles.english: Chapter: If somebody breaks the Saum thinking that the sun has set

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ الصِّبْيَانِ
titles.english: Chapter: Saum (fasting) of boys (children etc.)

titles.arabic: باب الْوِصَالِ، وَمَنْ قَالَ لَيْسَ فِي اللَّيْلِ صِيَامٌ
titles.english: Chapter: To observe Saum (fast) continuously

titles.arabic: باب التَّنْكِيلِ لِمَنْ أَكْثَرَ الْوِصَالَ
titles.english: Chapter: The punishment for the person who practises Al-Wisal very often.

titles.arabic: باب الْوِصَالِ إِلَى السَّحَرِ
titles.english: Chapter: To observe Saum continuously day and night (Al-Wisal) till the time of Sahar.

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَقْسَمَ عَلَى أَخِيهِ لِيُفْطِرَ فِي التَّطَوُّعِ وَلَمْ يَرَ عَلَيْهِ قَضَاءً، إِذَا كَانَ أَوْفَقَ لَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: If someone forces his Muslim brother to break his (Nawafil) fast.

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ شَعْبَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: Saum (fasting) in the month of Sha'ban.

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُذْكَرُ مِنْ صَوْمِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَإِفْطَارِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Fasting and non-fasting (periods) of the Prophet saws.

titles.arabic: باب حَقِّ الضَّيْفِ فِي الصَّوْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The right of the guest in fasting.

titles.arabic: باب حَقِّ الْجِسْمِ فِي الصَّوْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The right of the body in observing As-Saum.

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ الدَّهْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Observing Saum (fasting) daily throughout the life.

titles.arabic: باب حَقِّ الأَهْلِ فِي الصَّوْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The right of the family (wife) in observing As-Saum.

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ يَوْمٍ وَإِفْطَارِ يَوْمٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Saum (fasting) on alternate days

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ دَاوُدَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمُ
titles.english: Chapter: The Saum (fasting) of Da'wud (David) alayhis salam.

titles.arabic: باب صِيَامِ أَيَّامِ الْبِيضِ ثَلاَثَ عَشْرَةَ وَأَرْبَعَ عَشْرَةَ وَخَمْسَ عَشْرَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: To fast the 13th, 14th and 15th of the lunar months.

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ زَارَ قَوْمًا فَلَمْ يُفْطِرْ عِنْدَهُمْ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever visited some people and did not break his (optional) Saum (fast) with them

titles.arabic: باب الصَّوْمِ مِنْ آخِرَ الشَّهْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Fasting the last days of the month

titles.arabic: بَابُ صَوْمِ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ، فَإِذَا أَصْبَحَ صَائِمًا يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ فَعَلَيْهِ أَنْ يُفْطِرَ
titles.english: Chapter: Observing Saum (fast) on Friday

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يَخُصُّ شَيْئًا مِنَ الأَيَّامِ
titles.english: Chapter: Can one select some special days (for observing Saum (fast))?

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ يَوْمِ عَرَفَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: Observing Saum (fast) on the day of 'Arafah.

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ يَوْمِ الْفِطْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Observing Saum on the first day of 'Eid-ul-Fitr.

titles.arabic: باب الصَّوْمِ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Observing Saum on the first day of 'Eid-ul-Adha.

titles.arabic: باب صِيَامِ أَيَّامِ التَّشْرِيقِ
titles.english: Chapter: Observing Saum (fast) on Tashriq days.

titles.arabic: باب صِيَامِ يَوْمِ عَاشُورَاءَ
titles.english: Chapter: Observing Saum (fast) on the day of 'Ashura.

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ مَنْ قَامَ رَمَضَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Nawafil at night in Ramadan

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of the night of Qadr

titles.arabic: باب الْتِمَاسِ لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ فِي السَّبْعِ الأَوَاخِرِ
titles.english: Chapter: To look for the night of Qadr in the last seven nights (of Ramadan)

titles.arabic: باب تَحَرِّي لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ فِي الْوِتْرِ مِنَ الْعَشْرِ الأَوَاخِرِ
titles.english: Chapter: To search for the night of Qadr in the odd nights

titles.arabic: باب رَفْعِ مَعْرِفَةِ لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ لِتَلاَحِي النَّاسِ
titles.english: Chapter: The knowledge of the night of Qadr was taken away because of the quarrelling

titles.arabic: باب الْعَمَلِ فِي الْعَشْرِ الأَوَاخِرِ مِنْ رَمَضَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: Good deeds in the last ten days of Ramadan.

titles.arabic: باب الاِعْتِكَافِ فِي الْعَشْرِ الأَوَاخِرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The I'tikaf in the last ten days of Ramadan.

titles.arabic: باب الْحَائِضُ تُرَجِّلُ رَأْسَ الْمُعْتَكِفَ
titles.english: Chapter: A menstruating woman is permitted to comb the hair of a man in I'tikaf.

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَدْخُلُ الْبَيْتَ إِلاَّ لِحَاجَةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Mu'takif not to enter the house except for a need.

titles.arabic: باب غَسْلِ الْمُعْتَكِفِ
titles.english: Chapter: The taking of a bath by a Mu'takif.

titles.arabic: باب الاِعْتِكَافِ لَيْلاً
titles.english: Chapter: The I'tikaf at night.

titles.arabic: باب اعْتِكَافِ النِّسَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: Women's I'tikaf.

titles.arabic: باب الأَخْبِيَةِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ
titles.english: Chapter: Tents in mosque.

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يَخْرُجُ الْمُعْتَكِفُ لِحَوَائِجِهِ إِلَى باب الْمَسْجِدِ
titles.english: Chapter: Mu'takif going to the gate of the mosque.

titles.arabic: باب الاِعْتِكَافِ وَخَرَجَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم صَبِيحَةَ عِشْرِينَ
titles.english: Chapter: Coming out of I'tikaf in the morning of the twentieth (of Ramadan)

titles.arabic: باب اعْتِكَافِ الْمُسْتَحَاضَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: I'tikaf of a woman who has bleeding in between her periods

titles.arabic: باب زِيَارَةِ الْمَرْأَةِ زَوْجَهَا فِي اعْتِكَافِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: The visit of the wife to her husband in I'tikaf

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يَدْرَأُ الْمُعْتَكِفُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Is it permissible for the Mu'takif to defend himself.

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ خَرَجَ مِنَ اعْتِكَافِهِ عِنْدَ الصُّبْحِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever went out of his I'tikaf in the morning

titles.arabic: باب الاِعْتِكَافِ فِي شَوَّالٍ
titles.english: Chapter: I'tikaf in the month of Shawwal

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ عَلَيْهِ صَوْمًا إِذَا اعْتَكَفَ
titles.english: Chapter: I'tikaf without fasting

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا نَذَرَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ أَنْ يَعْتَكِفَ ثُمَّ أَسْلَمَ
titles.english: Chapter: Vow in the pre-Islamic period to perform I'tikaf

titles.arabic: باب الاِعْتِكَافِ فِي الْعَشْرِ الأَوْسَطِ مِنْ رَمَضَانَ
titles.english: Chapter: I'tikaf in the middle ten days of Ramadan

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَعْتَكِفَ ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever intended I'tikaf and then changed his mind

titles.arabic: باب الْمُعْتَكِفِ يُدْخِلُ رَأْسَهُ الْبَيْتَ لِلْغَسْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: A Mu'takif can let his head in the house for washing

titles.arabic: بَابُ مَا جَاءَ فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {فَإِذَا قُضِيَتِ الصَّلاَةُ فَانْتَشِرُوا فِي الأَرْضِ وَابْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِ اللَّهِ وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ كَثِيرًا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا انْفَضُّوا إِلَيْهَا وَتَرَكُوكَ قَائِمًا قُلْ مَا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ مِنَ اللَّهْوِ وَمِنَ التِّجَارَةِ وَاللَّهُ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ}
titles.english: Chapter: What has come in the Statement of Allah تعالى: “Then when the (Jumu‘ah) Salat is ended, you may disperse through the land, and seek of the Bounty of Allah.... And Allah is the Best of Providers.” (V.62:10,11)

titles.arabic: باب الْحَلاَلُ بَيِّنٌ وَالْحَرَامُ بَيِّنٌ وَبَيْنَهُمَا مُشَبَّهَاتٌ
titles.english: Chapter: Legal, illegal, and doubtful things

titles.arabic: باب تَفْسِيرِ الْمُشَبَّهَاتِ
titles.english: Chapter: Explanation of doubtful (unclear) things

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُتَنَزَّهُ مِنَ الشُّبُهَاتِ
titles.english: Chapter: What doubtful (unclear) things should be avoided?

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ الْوَسَاوِسَ وَنَحْوَهَا مِنَ الْمُشَبَّهَاتِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever does not consider dark suggestions

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا انْفَضُّوا إِلَيْهَا}
titles.english: Chapter: The Statement of Allah azza wajal: "And when they see some merchandise or some amusement they disperse headlong to it ..."

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يُبَالِ مِنْ حَيْثُ كَسَبَ الْمَالَ
titles.english: Chapter: One who does not care from where he earns

titles.arabic: باب التِّجَارَةِ فِي الْبَرِّ
titles.english: Chapter: Trade of cloth and other things

titles.arabic: باب الْخُرُوجِ فِي التِّجَارَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Going out for Trading

titles.arabic: باب التِّجَارَةِ فِي الْبَحْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Trading in sea

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا انْفَضُّوا إِلَيْهَا} وَقَوْلُهُ جَلَّ ذِكْرُهُ: {رِجَالٌ لاَ تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجَارَةٌ وَلاَ بَيْعٌ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ}
titles.english: Chapter: “And when they see some merchandise or some amusement [beating of Tambur (drum) etc.], they disperse headlong to it...” (V.62:11). And Allah’s Statement: “Men whom neither trade nor sale divert them from the Remembrance of Allah...” (V.24:37)

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {أَنْفِقُوا مِنْ طَيِّبَاتِ مَا كَسَبْتُمْ}
titles.english: Chapter: Allah's Statement: "... Spend of the good things which you have earned ..."

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَحَبَّ الْبَسْطَ فِي الرِّزْقِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever liked to expand in his sustenance
