titles.english: "And indeed We revealed to Musa (Moses) (saying): 'Travel by night with 'Ibâdi (My slaves) and strike a dry path for them in the sea, fearing neither to be overtaken [by Fir'aūn (Pharaoh)], nor being afraid (of drowning in the sea).' Then Fir'aūn (Pharaoh) pursued them with his hosts, but the sea-water completely overwhelmed them and covered them up. And Fir'aun (Pharaoh) led his people astray, and he did not guide them." (V.20:77-79)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏فَلاَ يُخْرِجَنَّكُمَا مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ فَتَشْقَى‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "...So let him not get you both out of Paradise, so that you be distressed." (V.20:117)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏كَمَا بَدَأْنَا أَوَّلَ خَلْقٍ نُّعِيدُهُ ۚ وَعْدًا عَلَيْنَا ۚ‏} ‏
titles.english: "As We began the first creation, We shall repeat it. (It is) a promise binding upon Us. Truly, We shall do it." (V.21:104)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَتَرَى النَّاسَ سُكَارَى‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High.: "...And you shall see mankind as in a drunken state..." (V.22:2)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَعْبُدُ اللَّهَ عَلَى حَرْفٍ فَإِنْ أَصَابَهُ خَيْرٌ اطْمَأَنَّ بِهِ وَإِنْ أَصَابَتْهُ فِتْنَةٌ انْقَلَبَ عَلَى وَجْهِهِ خَسِرَ الدُّنْيَا وَالآخِرَةَ‏} ‏ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏ذَلِكَ هُوَ الضَّلاَلُ الْبَعِيدُ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And among mankind is he who worships Allah as it were, upon the very edge (i.e., in doubt)..." (V.22:11)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏هَذَانِ خَصْمَانِ اخْتَصَمُوا فِي رَبِّهِمْ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "These two opponents (believers and disbelievers) dispute with each other about their Lord..." (V.22:19)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ‏ {‏وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ أَزْوَاجَهُمْ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُمْ شُهَدَاءُ إِلاَّ أَنْفُسُهُمْ فَشَهَادَةُ أَحَدِهِمْ أَرْبَعُ شَهَادَاتٍ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And for those who accuse their wives, but have no witnesses except themselves..." (V.24:6)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَالْخَامِسَةُ أَنَّ لَعْنَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ إِنْ كَانَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And the fifth (testimony should be) the invoking of the Curse of Allah on him if he is of those who tell a lie (against her) ." (V.24:7)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَيَدْرَأُ عَنْهَا الْعَذَابَ أَنْ تَشْهَدَ أَرْبَعَ شَهَادَاتٍ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "But it shall avert the punishment (of stoning to death) from her..." (V.24:8)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَالْخَامِسَةُ أَنَّ غَضَبَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا إِنْ كَانَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And the fifth (testimony) should be that the Wrath of Allah be upon her if he (her husband) speaks the truth." (V.24:9)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِنَّ الَّذِينَ جَاءُوا بِالإِفْكِ عُصْبَةٌ مِنْكُمْ لاَ تَحْسِبُوهُ شَرًّا لَكُمْ بَلْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ مَا اكْتَسَبَ مِنَ الإِثْمِ وَالَّذِي تَوَلَّى كِبْرَهُ مِنْهُمْ لَهُ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ‏} ‏ أَفَّاكٌ كَذَّابٌ‏.‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "Verily! Those who brought forth the slander (against 'Aishah [May Allah be pleased with her] the wife of the Prophet pbuh) are a group among you." (V.24:11)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏لَوْلاَ إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ ظَنَّ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ، وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتُ، بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ خَيْرًا وَقَالُوا هَذَا إِفْكٌ مُبِينٌ. لَّوْلَا جَاءُوا عَلَيْهِ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَاءَ ۚ فَإِذْ لَمْ يَأْتُوا بِالشُّهَدَاءِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ عِندَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْكَاذِبُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Why then did not the believers, men and women, when you heard it (the slander) think good of their own people and say: 'This (charge) is an obvious lie... (up to) ... Then with Allah they are the liars." (V.24: 12-13)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَلَوْلاَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالآخِرَةِ لَمَسَّكُمْ فِيمَا أَفَضْتُمْ فِيهِ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Had it not been for the Grace of Allah and His Mercy unto you in this world and in the Hereafter, a great torment would have touched you for that whereof you had spoken." (V.24:14)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِذْ تَلَقَّوْنَهُ بِأَلْسِنَتِكُمْ وَتَقُولُونَ بِأَفْوَاهِكُمْ مَا لَيْسَ لَكُمْ بِهِ عِلْمٌ وَتَحْسِبُونَهُ هَيِّنًا وَهْوَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَظِيمٌ‏} ‏
titles.english: "When you were propagating it with your tongues, and uttering with your mouths that whereof you had no knowledge..." (V.24:15)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏يَعِظُكُمُ اللَّهُ أَنْ تَعُودُوا لِمِثْلِهِ أَبَدًا‏} ‏ الآيَةَ
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And warns you not to repeat the like of it, forever." (V.24:17)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَيُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمُ الآيَاتِ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah: "And Allāh makes the Ayāt (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) plain to you. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise." (V.24:18)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحِبُّونَ أَنْ تَشِيعَ الْفَاحِشَةُ فِي الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالآخِرَةِ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنْتُمْ لاَ تَعْلَمُونَ * وَلَوْلاَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ رَءُوفٌ رَحِيمٌ‏} ‏ ‏{‏وَلاَ يَأْتَلِ أُولُو الْفَضْلِ مِنْكُمْ وَالسَّعَةِ أَنْ يُؤْتُوا أُولِي الْقُرْبَى وَالْمَسَاكِينَ وَالْمُهَاجِرِينَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَلْيَعْفُوا وَلْيَصْفَحُوا أَلاَ تُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ‏}‏‏ .‏
titles.english: "Verily, those who like that (the crime of) illegal sexual intercourse should be propagated about those who believe... (up to) ... and that Allah is full of kindness, Most Merciful." (V.24:19,20) "And let not those among you who are blessed with graces and wealth swear not to give (any sort of help) to their kinsmen, Al-Masākīn (the poor)... (up to) ... And Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful." (V.24:22)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَلْيَضْرِبْنَ بِخُمُرِهِنَّ عَلَى جُيُوبِهِنَّ‏} ‏
titles.english: "... and to draw their veils all over their Juyubihinna (i.e., their bodies, faces, necks and bosoms)..." (V.24:31)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏الَّذِينَ يُحْشَرُونَ عَلَى وُجُوهِهِمْ إِلَى جَهَنَّمَ أُولَئِكَ شَرٌّ مَكَانًا وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلاً‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Those who will be gathered to Hell (prone) on their faces..." (V.25:34)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَالَّذِينَ لاَ يَدْعُونَ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَهًا آخَرَ وَلاَ يَقْتُلُونَ النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلاَّ بِالْحَقِّ وَلاَ يَزْنُونَ وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذَلِكَ يَلْقَ أَثَامًا‏} ‏ الْعُقُوبَةَ
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And those who invoke not any other ilāh (God) along with Allah, nor kill such person..." (V.25:68)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏يُضَاعَفْ لَهُ الْعَذَابُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَيَخْلُدْ فِيهِ مُهَانًا‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "The torment will be doubled to him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein in disgrace." (V.25:69)

titles.arabic: باب ‏{‏إِلاَّ مَنْ تَابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ عَمَلاً صَالِحًا فَأُولَئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ اللَّهُ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ حَسَنَاتٍ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا‏}‏
titles.english: "Except those who repent and believe (in the Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous deeds, for those, Allah will change their sins into good deeds, and Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful." (V.25:70)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏فَسَوْفَ يَكُونُ لِزَامًا‏} ‏ هَلَكَةً
titles.english: . "... So the torment will be yours for ever" (V.25:77)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَلاَ تُخْزِنِي يَوْمَ يُبْعَثُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And disgrace me not on the day when (all the creatures) will be resurrected." (V.26:87)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَأَنْذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ الأَقْرَبِينَ * وَاخْفِضْ جَنَاحَكَ‏} ‏ أَلِنْ جَانِبَكَ
titles.english: "And warn your tribe (O Muḥammad pbuh) of near kindred. And be kind and humble to the believers who follow you.. ." (V.26:214-215)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِنَّكَ لاَ تَهْدِي مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشَاءُ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "Verily! You (O Muḥammad pbuh) guide not whom you like, but Allah guides whom He wills..." (V.28:56)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِنَّ الَّذِي فَرَضَ عَلَيْكَ الْقُرْآنَ‏} ‏ الآيَةَ
titles.english: "Verily, He Who has given you (O Muḥammad pbuh) the Qur'an..." (V.28:85)

titles.arabic: باب ‏{‏لاَ تُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ‏}‏
titles.english: "... Join not in worship others with Allah. Verily! Joining others in worship with Allah is a great Ẓulm (wrong) indeed." (V.31:13)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِنَّ اللَّهَ عِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "Verily, Allah! With Him (Alone) is the knowledge of the Hour..." (V.31:34)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏فَلاَ تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ مَا أُخْفِيَ لَهُمْ‏ مِّن قُرَّةِ أَعْيُنٍ} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy..." (V.32:17)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏ادْعُوهُمْ لآبَائِهِمْ هُوَ أَقْسَطُ عِندَ اللَّهِ ۚ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Call them (adopted sons) by (the names of) their fathers. That is more just with Allah..." (V.33:5)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَضَى نَحْبَهُ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَنْتَظِرُ وَمَا بَدَّلُوا تَبْدِيلاً‏} ‏
titles.english: "Of them, some have fulfilled their obligations (i.e ., have been martyred) and some of them are still waiting, but they have never changed (i.e., they never proved treacherous to their covenant which they concluded with Allah) in the least." (V.33:23)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لأَزْوَاجِكَ إِنْ كُنْتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتَهَا فَتَعَالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلاً‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah (the Exalted): "O Prophet! (Muḥammad pbuh)! Say to your wives: 'If you desire the life of this world and its glitter, - then come! I will make a provision for you and set you free in a handsome manner (divorce)'." (V.33:28)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَإِنْ كُنْتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَالدَّارَ الآخِرَةَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِنَاتِ مِنْكُنَّ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "But if you desire Allah and His Messenger, and the home of the Hereafter, then verily, Allah has prepared for Al-Muhsināt (good-doers) amongst you an enormous reward." (V.33:29)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللَّهُ مُبْدِيهِ وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشَاهُ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And (remember) when you said to him (Zaid bin Haritha [may Allah be pleased with him] - the freed slave of the Prophet pbuh) on whom Allah has bestowed grace (by guiding him to Islam) and you (O Muḥammad pbuh too) have done favour (by manumitting him): 'Keep your wife to yourself and fear Allah.' But you did hide in yourself (i.e., what Allah has already made known to you that He will give her to you in marriage) that which Allah will make manifest, you did fear people (i.e., Muḥammad pbuh married the divorced wife of his manumitted slave) whereas Allah had a better right that you should fear Him..." (V.33:37)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏{‏تُرْجِئُ مَنْ تَشَاءُ مِنْهُنَّ وَتُؤْوِي إِلَيْكَ مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَمَنِ ابْتَغَيْتَ مِمَّنْ عَزَلْتَ فَلاَ جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكَ‏}‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "You (O Muḥammad pbuh) can postpone (the turn of) whom you will of them (your wives), and you may receive whom you will. And whomsoever you desire of those whom you have set aside (her turn temporarily), it is no sin on you (to receive her again)..." (V.33:51)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏لاَ تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتَ النَّبِيِّ إِلاَّ أَنْ يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ إِلَى طَعَامٍ غَيْرَ نَاظِرِينَ إِنَاهُ وَلَكِنْ إِذَا دُعِيتُمْ فَادْخُلُوا فَإِذَا طَعِمْتُمْ فَانْتَشِرُوا وَلاَ مُسْتَأْنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ إِنَّ ذَلِكُمْ كَانَ يُؤْذِي النَّبِيَّ فَيَسْتَحْيِي مِنْكُمْ وَاللَّهُ لاَ يَسْتَحْيِي مِنَ الْحَقِّ وَإِذَا سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ مَتَاعًا فَاسْأَلُوهُنَّ مِنْ وَرَاءِ حِجَابٍ ذَلِكُمْ أَطْهَرُ لِقُلُوبِكُمْ وَقُلُوبِهِنَّ وَمَا كَانَ لَكُمْ أَنْ تُؤْذُوا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَلاَ أَنْ تَنْكِحُوا أَزْوَاجَهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ أَبَدًا إِنَّ ذَلِكُمْ كَانَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَظِيمًا‏}‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "...O you who believe! Enter not the Prophet's houses, except when leave is given to you for a meal... (up to) ... Verily! With Allah that shall be an enormity." (V.33:53)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏{‏إِنْ تُبْدُوا شَيْئًا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمًا * لاَ جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِي آبَائِهِنَّ وَلاَ أَبْنَائِهِنَّ وَلاَ إِخْوَانِهِنَّ وَلاَ أَبْنَاءِ إِخْوَانِهِنَّ وَلاَ أَبْنَاءِ أَخَوَاتِهِنَّ وَلاَ نِسَائِهِنَّ وَلاَ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُهُنَّ وَاتَّقِينَ اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدً‏}‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: “Whether you reveal anything or conceal it, verily, Allah is [the] Ever All-Knower of everything... (up to) ... Verily, Allah is Ever All-Witness over everything." (V-33:54,55)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَمَلاَئِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Allah sends His Salāt (Graces, Honours, Blessings and Mercy) on the Prophet (Muḥammad pbuh) and also His angels (ask Allah to bless and forgive him)..." (V.33:56)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏لاَ تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ آذَوْا مُوسَى‏} ‏
titles.english: "Be you not like those who annoyed Mūsa (Moses)." (V.33:69)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏حَتَّى إِذَا فُزِّعَ عَنْ قُلُوبِهِمْ، قَالُوا مَاذَا قَالَ رَبُّكُمْ قَالُوا الْحَقَّ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ‏} ‏
titles.english: "...So much so that when fear is banished from their (angels') hearts, they (angels) say: 'What is it that your Lord has said?' They say: 'The truth. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.'" (V.34:23).

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏{‏إِنْ هُوَ إِلاَّ نَذِيرٌ لَكُمْ بَيْنَ يَدَىْ عَذَابٍ شَدِيدٍ‏}‏
titles.english: "...He (Muḥammad pbuh) is only a warner to you in face of a severe torment." (V.34:46)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَالشَّمْسُ تَجْرِي لِمُسْتَقَرٍّ لَهَا ذَلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ‏} ‏
titles.english: Allah's Statement: "And the sun runs on its fixed course for a term (appointed). That is the Decree of the All Mighty, the All-Knowing." (V.36:38)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَإِنَّ يُونُسَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And, verily, Yūnus (Jonah) was one of the Messengers." (V.37:139)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏هَبْ لِي مُلْكًا لاَ يَنْبَغِي لأَحَدٍ مِنْ بَعْدِي إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْوَهَّابُ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "He (Solomon peace be upon him) said: "My Lord! Forgive me. And bestow upon me a kingdom such as shall not belong to any other after me. Verily, You are the Bestower." (V.38:35)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَمَا أَنَا مِنَ الْمُتَكَلِّفِينَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "...Nor am I one of the Mutakallifūn (those who pretend and fabricate things which do not exist) ." (V.38:86)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏يَا عِبَادِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ لاَ تَقْنَطُوا مِنْ رَحْمَةِ اللَّهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ جَمِيعًا إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: Say: "O 'Ibādi (My slaves) who have transgressed against themselves (by committing evil deeds and sins)! Despair not of the Mercy of Allah..." (V39:53)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَمَا قَدَرُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "They made not a just estimate of Allah such as is due to Him..." (V.39:67)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَالأَرْضُ جَمِيعًا قَبْضَتُهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَالسَّمَوَاتُ مَطْوِيَّاتٌ بِيَمِينِهِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "...And on the Day of Resurrection, the whole of the earth will be grasped by His Hand and the heavens will be rolled up in His Right Hand..." (V.39:67)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ فَصَعِقَ مَنْ فِي السَّمَوَاتِ وَمَنْ فِي الأَرْضِ إِلاَّ مَنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَى فَإِذَا هُمْ قِيَامٌ يَنْظُرُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "And the Trumpet will be blown, and all who are in the heavens and all who are on earth will swoon away, except him whom Allah wills..." (V.39:68)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَمَا كُنْتُمْ تَسْتَتِرُونَ أَنْ يَشْهَدَ عَلَيْكُمْ سَمْعُكُمْ وَلاَ أَبْصَارُكُمْ وَلاَ جُلُودُكُمْ وَلَكِنْ ظَنَنْتُمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ لاَ يَعْلَمُ كَثِيرًا مِمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ‏}
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "And you have not been hiding yourself (in the world), lest your ears, and your eyes, and your skins should testify against you..." (V.41:22)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَذَلِكُمْ ظَنُّكُمُ الَّذِي ظَنَنْتُمْ بِرَبِّكُمْ أَرْدَاكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُمْ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ‏}
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "And that thought of yours which you thought about your Lord, has brought you to destruction, and you have become (this Day) of those utterly lost!" (V.41:23)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِلاَّ الْمَوَدَّةَ فِي الْقُرْبَى‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "...Except to be kind to me for my kinship with you..." (V.42:23)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَنَادَوْا يَا مَالِكُ لِيَقْضِ عَلَيْنَا رَبُّكَ‏} الآيَةَ
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And they will cry: "O Mālik (Keeper of Hell)! Let your Lord make an end of us' He will say, 'Verily, you shall abide forever.'" (V.43:77)

titles.arabic: بَابُ ‏ {‏أَفَنَضْرِبُ عَنْكُمُ الذِّكْرَ صَفْحًا أَنْ كُنْتُمْ قَوْمًا مُسْرِفِينَ‏} ‏ مُشْرِكِينَ‏.
titles.english: "Shall we then (warn you not and) take away the Reminder (this Qur'an) from you, because you are a people Musrifun." (1) (V.43:5) (1) (Chap. 2) Musrifun: These who transgress the limits, as sinners, polytheists, idolaters, and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah and in His Messenger Muḥammad peace be upon him.

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏يَوْمَ تَأْتِي السَّمَاءُ بِدُخَانٍ مُبِينٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Then wait you for the Day when the sky will bring forth a visible smoke." (V.44:10)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏يَغْشَى النَّاسَ هَذَا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Covering the people, this is a painful torment." (V.44:11)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏رَبَّنَا اكْشِفْ عَنَّا الْعَذَابَ إِنَّا مُؤْمِنُونَ‏}
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "(They will say) Our Lord! Remove the torment from us, really we shall become believers!" (V.44:12)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏أَنَّى لَهُمُ الذِّكْرَى وَقَدْ جَاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مُبِينٌ‏} ‏
titles.english: "How can there be for them an admonition (at the time when the torment has reached them), when a Messenger explaining things clearly, has already come to them?" (V.44:13)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏ثُمَّ تَوَلَّوْا عَنْهُ وَقَالُوا مُعَلَّمٌ مَجْنُونٌ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Then they had turned away from him (Messenger Muḥammad PBUH) and said: (He is) taught (by a human being), a madman!" (V.44:14)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏يَوْمَ نَبْطِشُ الْبَطْشَةَ الْكُبْرَى إِنَّا مُنْتَقِمُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "On the Day when We shall seize you with the greatest seizure (punishment). Verily, We will exact retribution." (V.44:16)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَالَّذِي قَالَ لِوَالِدَيْهِ أُفٍّ لَكُمَا أَتَعِدَانِنِي أَنْ أُخْرَجَ وَقَدْ خَلَتِ الْقُرُونُ مِنْ قَبْلِي وَهُمَا يَسْتَغِيثَانِ اللَّهَ وَيْلَكَ آمِنْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ فَيَقُولُ مَا هَذَا إِلاَّ أَسَاطِيرُ الأَوَّلِينَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "But he who says to his parents: Fie upon you both! Do you hold out the promise to me that I shall be raised up (again)... (up to) ... the tales of the ancient." (V.46:17)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ عَارِضًا مُسْتَقْبِلَ أَوْدِيَتِهِمْ قَالُوا هَذَا عَارِضٌ مُمْطِرُنَا بَلْ هُوَ مَا اسْتَعْجَلْتُمْ بِهِ رِيحٌ فِيهَا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Then, when they saw it as a dense cloud coming towards their valleys..." (V.46:24)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَتُقَطِّعُوا أَرْحَامَكُمْ‏} ‏
titles.english: "...And sever your ties of kinship." (V.47:22)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِنَّا فَتَحْنَا لَكَ فَتْحًا مُبِينًا‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Verily, We have given you (O Muḥammad pbuh) a manifest victory." (V.48:1)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللَّهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ وَيُتِمَّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكَ وَيَهْدِيَكَ صِرَاطًا مُسْتَقِيمًا‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "That Allah may forgive you your sins of the past and the future and complete His Favour on you and guide you on the Straight Path." (V.48:2)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ شَاهِدًا وَمُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا‏} ‏
titles.english: "Verily, We have sent you (O Muḥammad pbuh) as a witness, as a bearer of glad tidings, and as a warner." (V.48:8)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏هُوَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ السَّكِينَةَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "He it is Who sent down As-Sakīnah (tranquillity and calmness) into the hearts of the believers..." (V.48:4)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِذْ يُبَايِعُونَكَ تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "...When they gave their Bai'a (pledge) to you (O Muḥammad pbuh) under the tree..." (V.48:18)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏لاَ تَرْفَعُوا أَصْوَاتَكُمْ فَوْقَ صَوْتِ النَّبِيِّ‏} ‏ الآيَةَ
titles.english: "O you who believe! Raise not your voices above the voice of the Prophet pbuh ..." (V.49:2)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُنَادُونَكَ مِنْ وَرَاءِ الْحُجُرَاتِ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لاَ يَعْقِلُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Verily! Those who call you from behind the dwellings, most of them have no sense." (V.49:4)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَتَقُولُ هَلْ مِنْ مَزِيدٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: Allah's Statement: "...It (Hell) will say: 'Are there any more (to come)?" (V.50:30)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ الْغُرُوبِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "...And glorify the praises of your Lord, before the rising of the sun and before (its) setting (i.e., the Fajr, Ẓuhr and 'Aṣr prayers) ." (V.50:39)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ اللاَّتَ وَالْعُزَّى‏} ‏
titles.english: "Have you then considered Al-Lat and AI-'Uzza? (1) " (V.53:19) (1) :(Ch. 2) Lat and 'Uzza were two idols worshipped by the pagan Arabs during the Pre-Islamic Period of Ignorance.

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَمَنَاةَ الثَّالِثَةَ الأُخْرَى‏} ‏
titles.english: "And Manat (another idol of the pagan Arabs) the other third." (V.53:20)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏فَاسْجُدُوا لِلَّهِ وَاعْبُدُوا‏} ‏
titles.english: "So, fall you down in prostration to Allah, and worship Him (Alone) ." (V.53:62)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَانْشَقَّ الْقَمَرُ * وَإِنْ يَرَوْا آيَةً يُعْرِضُوا‏} ‏
titles.english: "...And the moon has been cleft asunder (the people of Makkah requested Prophet Muḥammad pbuh to show them a miracle, so he showed them the splitting of the moon). And if they see a sign, they turn away..." (V.54:1,2)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏تَجْرِي بِأَعْيُنِنَا جَزَاءً لِمَنْ كَانَ كُفِرَ* وَلَقَدْ تَرَكْنَاهَا آيَةً فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Floating under Our Eyes, a reward for him who had been rejected! " (V.54:14)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏فَكَانُوا كَهَشِيمِ الْمُحْتَظِرِ * وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: "... And they became like the dry stubble of a fold-builder. And indeed, We have made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition) ." (V.54:31,32)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَلَقَدْ صَبَّحَهُمْ بُكْرَةً عَذَابٌ مُسْتَقِرٌّ * فَذُوقُوا عَذَابِي وَنُذُرِ‏}
titles.english: "And verily, an abiding torment seized them early in the morning. Then, taste you My Torment and My Warnings." (V.54:38,39)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏سَيُهْزَمُ الْجَمْعُ وَيُوَلُّونَ الدُّبُرَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Their multitude will be put to flight." (V.54:45)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏بَلِ السَّاعَةُ مَوْعِدُهُمْ وَالسَّاعَةُ أَدْهَى وَأَمَرُّ‏} ‏ يَعْنِي مِنَ الْمَرَارَةِ
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Nay, but the Hour is their appointed time (for their full recompense), and the Hour will be more grievous and more bitter." (V.54:46)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَمِنْ دُونِهِمَا جَنَّتَانِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "And besides these two, there are two other gardens (i.e., in Paradise) ." (V.55:62)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏حُورٌ مَقْصُورَاتٌ فِي الْخِيَامِ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Ḥūr (beautiful fair females) guarded in pavilions." (V.55:72)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَظِلٍّ مَمْدُودٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And in shade long extended." (V.56:30)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏مَا قَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ لِينَةٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "What you (O Muslims) cut down of the palm-trees (of the enemy)..." (V.59:5)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلُهُ ‏ {‏مَا أَفَاءَ اللَّهُ عَلَى رَسُولِهِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "What Allah gave as booty (Fai') to His Messenger . . ." (V.59:7)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَمَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And whatsoever the Messenger (Muḥammad pbuh) gives you take it..." (V.59:7)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَالَّذِينَ تَبَوَّءُوا الدَّارَ وَالإِيمَانَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And (it is also for) those who, before them, had homes (in Al-Madīna) and had adopted the Faith..." (V.59:9)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَيُؤْثِرُونَ عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ‏} ‏ الآيَةَ
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "...And give them (emigrants) preference over themselves..." (V.59:9)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏لاَ تَتَّخِذُوا عَدُوِّي وَعَدُوَّكُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "(O you who believe!) Take not My enemies and your enemies (i.e., disbelievers and polytheists) as friends..." (V.60:1)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِذَا جَاءَكُمُ الْمُؤْمِنَاتُ مُهَاجِرَاتٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "...When believing women come to you as emigrants..." (V.60:10)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏إِذَا جَاءَكَ الْمُؤْمِنَاتُ يُبَايِعْنَكَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "O Prophet! When believing women come to you to give you the Bai'a (pledge).. ." (V.60:12)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى ‏ {‏مِنْ بَعْدِي اسْمُهُ أَحْمَدُ‏} ‏
titles.english: "[And (remember) when 'Isā (Jesus a.s.), son of Mary said: "O Children of Israel! I am the Messenger of Allāh unto you, confirming the Torah (which came) before me, and giving glad tidings of a Messenger to come] after me, whose name shall be Ahmad." (61:6)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلُهُ ‏ {‏وَآخَرِينَ مِنْهُمْ لَمَّا يَلْحَقُوا بِهِمْ‏} ‏‏.‏ وَقَرَأَ عُمَرُ فَامْضُوا إِلَى ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ
titles.english: The Statement of Allah ta'ala: "And [He has sent him (Prophet Muḥammad pbuh) also to] others among them (Muslims) who have not yet joined them..." (V.62:3)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَإِذَا رَأَوْا تِجَارَةً‏} ‏
titles.english: "And when they see some merchandise or some amusement..." (V.62:11)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِذَا جَاءَكَ الْمُنَافِقُونَ قَالُوا نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ‏} ‏ إِلَى ‏{‏لَكَاذِبُونَ‏}‏‏.‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allāh the Most High: "When the hypocrites come to you (O Muḥammad pbuh) they say: 'We bear witness that you are indeed the Messenger of Allah..." (V.63:1)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمَانَهُمْ جُنَّةً‏} ‏ يَجْتَنُّونَ بِهَا
titles.english: "They have made their oaths a screen (for their hypocrisy)." (1) (63:2) (1): (Ch. 2) "An-Nifāq": (Hypocrisy is of two types, namely: A - Hypocrisy in Belief B - Hypocrisy in deeds and actions. )

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا فَطُبِعَ عَلَى قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لاَ يَفْقَهُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah ta'ala: "That is because they believed, then disbelieved, therefore their hearts are sealed, so they understand not." (V.63:3)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِذَا جَاءَكَ الْمُنَافِقُونَ قَالُوا نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ‏} ‏ إِلَى ‏{‏لَكَاذِبُونَ‏}‏‏.‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allāh the Most High: "When the hypocrites come to you (O Muḥammad pbuh) they say: 'We bear witness that you are indeed the Messenger of Allah..." (V.63:1)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏اتَّخَذُوا أَيْمَانَهُمْ جُنَّةً‏} ‏ يَجْتَنُّونَ بِهَا
titles.english: "They have made their oaths a screen (for their hypocrisy)." (1) (63:2) (1): (Ch. 2) "An-Nifāq": (Hypocrisy is of two types, namely: A - Hypocrisy in Belief B - Hypocrisy in deeds and actions. )

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا فَطُبِعَ عَلَى قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لاَ يَفْقَهُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah ta'ala: "That is because they believed, then disbelieved, therefore their hearts are sealed, so they understand not." (V.63:3)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَإِذَا رَأَيْتَهُمْ تُعْجِبُكَ أَجْسَامُهُمْ وَإِنْ يَقُولُوا تَسْمَعْ لِقَوْلِهِمْ كَأَنَّهُمْ خُشُبٌ مُسَنَّدَةٌ يَحْسِبُونَ كُلَّ صَيْحَةٍ عَلَيْهِمْ هُمُ الْعَدُوُّ فَاحْذَرْهُمْ قَاتَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ أَنَّى يُؤْفَكُونَ‏}
titles.english: "And when you look at them, their bodies please you, and when they speak, you listen to their words." (V.63:4) (3 م)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا يَسْتَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ لَوَّوْا رُءُوسَهُمْ وَرَأَيْتَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ وَهُمْ مُسْتَكْبِرُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "And when it is said to them: 'Come, so that the Messenger of Allah may ask forgiveness from Allah for you,' they turn aside their heads, and you would see them turning away their faces in pride." (V.63:5)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ لَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ لَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لاَ يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفَاسِقِينَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "It is equal to them whether you (Muḥammad pbuh) ask for their forgiveness.. ." (V.63:6)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏هُمُ الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ لاَ تُنْفِقُوا عَلَى مَنْ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ حَتَّى يَنْفَضُّوا‏} ‏ يَنْفَضُّوا} وَيَتَفَرَّقُوا
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "They are the ones who say: 'Spend not on those who are with Allah's Messenger, until they desert him..." (V.63:7)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏{‏يَقُولُونَ لَئِنْ رَجَعْنَا إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ لَيُخْرِجَنَّ الأَعَزُّ مِنْهَا الأَذَلَّ وَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلَكِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ‏}‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah ta'ala: "They (hypocrites) say: 'If we return to Al- Madīna, indeed the more honourable will expel therefrom the meaner..." (V.63:8)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَأُولاَتُ الأَحْمَالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَنْ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ يُسْرًا‏} ‏‏.‏ وَأُولاَتُ الأَحْمَالِ وَاحِدُهَا ذَاتُ حَمْلٍ
titles.english: . "... And for those who are pregnant (whether they are divorced or their husbands are dead), their 'Idda (prescribed period) is until they lay down their burdens, and whoever keeps his duty to Allah and fears Him, He will make his matter easy for him." (V.65:4)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ لَكَ تَبْتَغِي مَرْضَاةَ أَزْوَاجِكَ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ‏} ‏
titles.english: "O Prophet! Why do you forbid (for yourself) that which Allah has allowed to you?..." (V.66:1)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏تَبْتَغِي مَرْضَاةَ أَزْوَاجِكَ‏} ‏ ‏ {‏قَدْ فَرَضَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ تَحِلَّةَ أَيْمَانِكُمْ‏} ‏
titles.english: "... seeking to please your wives..." (V.66:1) "Allah has already ordained for you (O men), the dissolution of your oaths.. ." (V.66:2)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَإِذْ أَسَرَّ النَّبِيُّ إِلَى بَعْضِ أَزْوَاجِهِ حَدِيثًا فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَتْ بِهِ وَأَظْهَرَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ عَرَّفَ بَعْضَهُ وَأَعْرَضَ عَنْ بَعْضٍ فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَهَا بِهِ قَالَتْ مَنْ أَنْبَأَكَ هَذَا قَالَ نَبَّأَنِيَ الْعَلِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And (remember) when the Prophet pbuh disclosed a matter in confidence to one of his wives (Ḥafsa) (up to) ... The All-Aware." (V.66:3)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِنْ تَتُوبَا إِلَى اللَّهِ فَقَدْ صَغَتْ قُلُوبُكُمَا‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "If you two (wives of the Prophet, namely, 'Aishah and Hafsa) turn in repentance to Allah, (it will be better for you), your hearts are indeed so inclined (to oppose what the Prophet likes)." (V.66:4)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏عَسَى رَبُّهُ إِنْ طَلَّقَكُنَّ أَنْ يُبَدِّلَهُ أَزْوَاجًا خَيْرًا مِنْكُنَّ مُسْلِمَاتٍ مُؤْمِنَاتٍ قَانِتَاتٍ تَائِبَاتٍ عَابِدَاتٍ سَائِحَاتٍ ثَيِّبَاتٍ وَأَبْكَارًا‏} ‏
titles.english: "It may be, if he divorced you (all), that his Lord (Allah) will give him instead of you, wives better than you... (V.66:5)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏عُتُلٍّ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ زَنِيمٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Cruel, and moreover base-born (of illegitimate birth) ." (V.68:13)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَنْ سَاقٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: "(Remember) the Day when the Shin shalt be laid bare..." (V.68:42)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَدًّا وَلاَ سُوَاعًا وَلاَ يَغُوثَ وَيَعُوقَ‏} ‏
titles.english: "Nor shall you leave Wadd nor Suwā' nor Yagūth nor Ya'ūq nor Nasr'. . ." (V.71:23)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلُهُ ‏ {‏قُمْ فَأَنْذِرْ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "Arise and warn." (V.74:3)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَرَبَّكَ فَكَبِّرْ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "And magnify your Lord (Allah)!" (V.74:3)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَثِيَابَكَ فَطَهِّرْ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And purify your garments!" (V.74:4)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَالرِّجْزَ فَاهْجُرْ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And keep away from Ar-Rujz (the idols)" (V.74:5)

titles.arabic: باب وَقَوْلُهُ ‏ {‏لاَ تُحَرِّكْ بِهِ لِسَانَكَ لِتَعْجَلَ بِهِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "Move not your tongue concerning (the Qur'an, O Muḥammad pbuh) to make haste therewith." (V.75:16)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏فَإِذَا قَرَأْنَاهُ فَاتَّبِعْ قُرْآنَهُ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And when We have recited it to you [O Muḥammad PBUH through Jibril (Gabriel) a.s.], then follow its (the Qur'an's) recital." (V.75:18)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏إِنَّهَا تَرْمِي بِشَرَرٍ كَالْقَصْرِ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah ta'ala: "Verily! It (Hell) throws sparks (huge) as Al-Qasr (a fort or a huge log of wood) ." (V.77:32)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏{‏كَأَنَّهُ جِمَالاَتٌ صُفْرٌ‏}‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "As if they were yellow camels or bundles of ropes." (V.77:33)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏هَذَا يَوْمُ لاَ يَنْطِقُونَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "That will be a Day when they shall not speak (during some part of it) ." (V.77:35)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏يَوْمَ يُنْفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ فَتَأْتُونَ أَفْوَاجًا‏} ‏ زُمَرًا
titles.english: "The Day when the Trumpet will be blown, and you shall come forth in crowds (groups after groups) ." (V.78:18)

titles.arabic: بَابٌ {يَوْمَ يَقُومُ النَّاسُ لِرَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ}
titles.english: "The Day when (all) mankind will stand before the Lord of the 'Alamīn (mankind, jinn and all that exists)" (V.83:6)

titles.arabic: باب {فَسَوْفَ يُحَاسَبُ حِسَابًا يَسِيرًا}
titles.english: "He surely will receive an easy reckoning." (V.84:8)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏لَتَرْكَبُنَّ طَبَقًا عَنْ طَبَقٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: "You shall certainly travel from stage to stage (in this life and in the Hereafter)" (V.84:19)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَالنَّهَارِ إِذَا تَجَلَّى‏} ‏
titles.english: "By the day as it appears in brightness." (V.92:2)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَمَا خَلَقَ الذَّكَرَ وَالأُنْثَى‏} ‏
titles.english: "By Him Who created male and female." (V.92:3)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلُهُ ‏ {‏فَأَمَّا مَنْ أَعْطَى وَاتَّقَى‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "As for him who gives (in charity) and keeps his duty to Allah and fears Him." (V.92:5)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْيُسْرَى‏} ‏
titles.english: "We will make smooth for him the path of ease (goodness) ." (V.92:7)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَأَمَّا مَنْ بَخِلَ وَاسْتَغْنَى‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "But he who is [a] greedy miser and thinks himself self-sufficient." (V.92:8)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَكَذَّبَ بِالْحُسْنَى‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "And belies Al-Ḥusnā ..." (V.92:9).

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلْعُسْرَى‏} ‏
titles.english: "We will make smooth for him the path for evil." (V.92:10)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلُهُ ‏ {‏مَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَى‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Your Lord (O Muḥammad pbuh) has neither forsaken you nor hates you." (V.93:3)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلُهُ ‏ {‏مَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَى‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "Your Lord (O Muḥammad pbuh) has neither forsaken you nor hates you." (V.93:1-3)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏خَلَقَ الإِنْسَانَ مِنْ عَلَقٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "He has created man from a clot." (V.96:2)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏اقْرَأْ وَرَبُّكَ الأَكْرَمُ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Read! And your Lord is the Most Generous." (V.96:3)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏كَلاَّ لَئِنْ لَمْ يَنْتَهِ لَنَسْفَعَنْ بِالنَّاصِيَةِ * نَاصِيَةٍ كَاذِبَةٍ خَاطِئَةٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "Nay! If he (Abu Jahl) ceases not, We will catch him by the forelock, a lying sinful forelock!" (V.96:15,16)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "So whosoever does good equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant), shall see it." (V.99:7)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ‏} ‏
titles.english: "And whosoever does evil equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant), shall see it." (V.99:8)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَرَأَيْتَ النَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ أَفْوَاجًا‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "And you see that the people enter Allah's religion (Islām) in crowds." (V.110:2)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ إِنَّهُ كَانَ تَوَّابًا‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Exalted: "So, glorify the praises of your Lord, and ask His forgiveness. Verily! He is the One Who accepts the repentance and forgives." (V.110:3)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏وَتَبَّ * مَا أَغْنَى عَنْهُ مَالُهُ وَمَا كَسَبَ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "... and perish he! His wealth and his children will not benefit him!" (V.111:1-2)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏سَيَصْلَى نَارًا ذَاتَ لَهَبٍ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah the Most High: "He (Abu Lahab) will be burnt in a Fire of blazing flames!" (V.111:3)

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَامْرَأَتُهُ حَمَّالَةُ الْحَطَبِ‏}
titles.english: "And his wife too, who carries wood." (V.111:4)

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ ‏ {‏اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ‏} ‏
titles.english: The Statement of Allah ta'ala: "Allāh-uṣ-Ṣamad (The Self-Sufficient Master, Whom all creatures need, He neither eats nor drinks) ." (V.112:2)

titles.arabic: باب كَيْفَ نُزُولُ الْوَحْىِ وَأَوَّلُ مَا نَزَلَ
titles.english: Chapter: How the Divine Revelation used to be revealed and what was the first thing revealed

titles.arabic: باب نَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ بِلِسَانِ قُرَيْشٍ وَالْعَرَبِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Qur'an was revealed in the language of Quraish and the Arabs

titles.arabic: باب جَمْعِ الْقُرْآنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The collection of the Qur'an

titles.arabic: باب كَاتِبِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
titles.english: Chapter: The scribe of the Prophet (saws)

titles.arabic: باب أُنْزِلَ الْقُرْآنُ عَلَى سَبْعَةِ أَحْرُفٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The Qur'an was revealed to be recited in seven different ways

titles.arabic: باب تَأْلِيفِ الْقُرْآنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The compilation of the Qur'an

titles.arabic: باب كَانَ جِبْرِيلُ يَعْرِضُ الْقُرْآنَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
titles.english: Chapter: Jibril used to present the Qur'an to the Prophet (saws)

titles.arabic: باب الْقُرَّاءِ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم
titles.english: Chapter: The Qurra from among the Companions of the Prophet (saws)

titles.arabic: باب ‏‏فَضْلِ‏‏ فَاتِحَةِ الْكِتَابِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Fatiha-til-Kitab

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلُ سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Surat Al-Baqarah (No.2)

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلُ الْكَهْفِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Surat Al-Kahf (No.18)

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلُ سُورَةِ الْفَتْحِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Surat Al-Fath (No48)

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Qul-Huwa Allahu Ahad (Surat Al-Ikhlas) (No.112)

titles.arabic: ‏باب فَضْلِ‏‏ الْمُعَوِّذَاتِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of Al-Mu’awwidhat (Surat Al-Falaq and Surat An-Nas) (No.113 & 114)

titles.arabic: باب نُزُولِ السَّكِينَةِ وَالْمَلاَئِكَةِ عِنْدَ قِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The descent of As-Sakinah and angels at the time of the recitation of the Qur'an

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ لَمْ يَتْرُكِ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِلاَّ مَا بَيْنَ الدَّفَّتَيْنِ‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever said that the Prophet (saws) did not leave anything after his death

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْقُرْآنِ عَلَى سَائِرِ الْكَلاَمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of the Qur'an above other kinds of speech

titles.arabic: باب الْوَصَاةِ بِكِتَابِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ
titles.english: Chapter: To recommend the Book of Allah عز وجل

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَتَغَنَّ بِالْقُرْآنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever does not recite the Qur'an in a pleasant tone

titles.arabic: باب اغْتِبَاطِ صَاحِبِ الْقُرْآنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Wish to be the like of the one who recites the Qur'an

titles.arabic: باب خَيْرُكُمْ مَنْ تَعَلَّمَ الْقُرْآنَ وَعَلَّمَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: The best among you are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it

titles.arabic: باب الْقِرَاءَةِ عَنْ ظَهْرِ الْقَلْبِ
titles.english: Chapter: The recitation of the Qur'an by heart

titles.arabic: باب اسْتِذْكَارِ الْقُرْآنِ وَتَعَاهُدِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: The learning of the Qur'an by heart and the reciting of it repeatedly

titles.arabic: باب الْقِرَاءَةِ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The recitation of the Qur'an on an animal

titles.arabic: باب تَعْلِيمِ الصِّبْيَانِ الْقُرْآنَ
titles.english: Chapter: Teaching the Qur'an to the children

titles.arabic: بَابُ نِسْيَانِ الْقُرْآنِ وَهَلْ يَقُولُ نَسِيتُ آيَةَ كَذَا وَكَذَا
titles.english: Chapter: Forgetting the Qur'an. And can one say: “I forgot such and such a Verse?

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَرَ بَأْسًا أَنْ يَقُولَ سُورَةُ الْبَقَرَةِ وَسُورَةُ كَذَا وَكَذَا
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever thinks that there is no harm in saying: Surat Al-Baqarah or Surat so-and-so

titles.arabic: باب التَّرْتِيلِ فِي الْقِرَاءَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The recitation of Qur'an in Tartil

titles.arabic: باب مَدِّ الْقِرَاءَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Prolonging certain sounds while reciting the Qur'an

titles.arabic: باب التَّرْجِيعِ
titles.english: Chapter: At-Tarji’

titles.arabic: باب حُسْنِ الصَّوْتِ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ لِلْقُرْآنِ
titles.english: Chapter: To recite the Qur'an in a charming voice

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَحَبَّ أَنْ يَسْمَعَ الْقُرْآنَ مِنْ غَيْرِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever likes to hear the Qur'an from another person

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ الْمُقْرِئِ لِلْقَارِئِ حَسْبُكَ
titles.english: Chapter: The saying of the listener to the reciter: “Enough!”

titles.arabic: بَابٌ في كَمْ يُقْرَأُ الْقُرْآنُ
titles.english: Chapter: What is the proper period for reciting the whole Qur'an

titles.arabic: باب الْبُكَاءِ عِنْدَ قِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ
titles.english: Chapter: To weep while reciting the Qur'an

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ رَايَا بِقِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ أَوْ تَأَكَّلَ بِهِ أَوْ فَخَرَ بِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: The sin of the person who recites the Qur'an to show off or to gain some worldly benefit, or to feel proud etc.

titles.arabic: باب اقْرَءُوا الْقُرْآنَ مَا ائْتَلَفَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ
titles.english: Chapter: Recite the Qur'an together as long as you agree about its interpretation

titles.arabic: باب التَّرْغِيبُ فِي النِّكَاحِ
titles.english: Chapter: Awakening the desire for marriage

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ مِنْكُمُ الْبَاءَةَ فَلْيَتَزَوَّجْ، لأَنَّهُ أَغَضُّ لِلْبَصَرِ وَأَحْصَنُ لِلْفَرْجِ»، وَهَلْ يَتَزَوَّجُ مَنْ لاَ أَرَبَ لَهُ فِي النِّكَاحِ
titles.english: Chapter: "Whoever is able to marry, should marry..."

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعِ الْبَاءَةَ فَلْيَصُمْ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever is not able to marry, is recommended to fast

titles.arabic: باب كَثْرَةِ النِّسَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: About (marrying) several women

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ هَاجَرَ أَوْ عَمِلَ خَيْرًا لِتَزْوِيجِ امْرَأَةٍ فَلَهُ مَا نَوَى
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever emigrated with the intention of marrying a woman

titles.arabic: باب تَزْوِيجِ الْمُعْسِرِ الَّذِي مَعَهُ الْقُرْآنُ وَالإِسْلاَمُ
titles.english: Chapter: The marrying of a poor man

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ الرَّجُلِ لأَخِيهِ انْظُرْ أَىَّ زَوْجَتَىَّ شِئْتَ حَتَّى أَنْزِلَ لَكَ عَنْهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The saying of a man to his brother (in Islam)

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنَ التَّبَتُّلِ وَالْخِصَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: What is disliked of not marrying and of getting castrated

titles.arabic: باب نِكَاحِ الأَبْكَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: To marry virgins

titles.arabic: باب الثَّيِّبَاتِ
titles.english: Chapter: The marrying of matrons

titles.arabic: باب تَزْوِيجِ الصِّغَارِ مِنَ الْكِبَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: The marrying of young lady to an elderly man

titles.arabic: باب إِلَى مَنْ يَنْكِحُ‏
titles.english: Chapter: What type of women should one seek in marriage?

titles.arabic: باب اتِّخَاذِ السَّرَارِيِّ
titles.english: Chapter: Having female captives and manumitting one's own slave-girl

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ جَعَلَ عِتْقَ الأَمَةِ صَدَاقَهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The manumission of a slave-girl as her Mahr (13b)

titles.arabic: بَابُ تَزْوِيجِ الْمُعْسِرِ لِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {إِنْ يَكُونُوا فُقَرَاءَ يُغْنِهِمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ}
titles.english: Chapter: "If they be poor, Allah will enrich them out of His Bounty."

titles.arabic: باب الأَكْفَاءِ فِي الدِّينِ
titles.english: Chapter: Husband and wife should have the same religion

titles.arabic: باب الأَكْفَاءِ فِي الْمَالِ، وَتَزْوِيجِ الْمُقِلِّ الْمُثْرِيَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: The marriage of a poor man with a well-to-do lady

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُتَّقَى مِنْ شُؤْمِ الْمَرْأَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: What evil omen of a lady is to be warded off

titles.arabic: باب الْحُرَّةِ تَحْتَ الْعَبْدِ
titles.english: Chapter: (About) a free lady as the wife of a slave

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَتَزَوَّجُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ أَرْبَعٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Not to marry more than four (at a time)

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَأُمَّهَاتُكُمُ اللاَّتِي أَرْضَعْنَكُمْ}
titles.english: Chapter: "..your foster-mothers who gave you suck."

titles.arabic: بَابُ مَنْ قَالَ لاَ رَضَاعَ بَعْدَ حَوْلَيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: "No suckling is to be carried on after the baby is two years old."

titles.arabic: باب لَبَنِ الْفَحْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: The milk belongs to the husband

titles.arabic: باب شَهَادَةِ الْمُرْضِعَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The witness of a wet nurse

titles.arabic: باب مَا يَحِلُّ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ وَمَا يَحْرُمُ
titles.english: Chapter: "Forbidden to you (for marriage) are: your mothers, your daughters..."

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَرَبَائِبُكُمُ اللاَّتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ مِنْ نِسَائِكُمُ اللاَّتِي دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ}
titles.english: Chapter: "....your step-daughters under your guardianship, born of your wives..."

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الأُخْتَيْنِ إِلاَّ مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ}
titles.english: Chapter: "(It is prohibited to have) two sisters in wedlock at the same time..."

titles.arabic: باب لاَ تُنْكَحُ الْمَرْأَةُ عَلَى عَمَّتِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: A woman should not marry a man already married to her paternal aunt

titles.arabic: باب الشِّغَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: Ash-Shighar. (Exchange of daughters or sisters in marriage without paying Mahr)

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ لِلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ تَهَبَ نَفْسَهَا لأَحَدٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Is it permissible for a woman to present herself for marriage to somebody?

titles.arabic: باب نِكَاحِ الْمُحْرِمِ
titles.english: Chapter: The marriage of Muhrim

titles.arabic: باب نَهْىِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم عَنْ نِكَاحِ الْمُتْعَةِ آخِرًا
titles.english: Chapter: Allah's Messenger (saws) prohibited Nikah-al-Mut'a lately.

titles.arabic: باب عَرْضِ الْمَرْأَةِ نَفْسَهَا عَلَى الرَّجُلِ الصَّالِحِ
titles.english: Chapter: A woman can present herself to a righteous man (for marriage)

titles.arabic: باب عَرْضِ الإِنْسَانِ ابْنَتَهُ أَوْ أُخْتَهُ عَلَى أَهْلِ الْخَيْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: The presentation of one's own daughter or sister (for marriage) to a religious man.

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ اللَّهِ جَلَّ وَعَزَّ : {وَلاَ جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا عَرَّضْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ خِطْبَةِ النِّسَاءِ أَوْ أَكْنَنْتُمْ فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ عَلِمَ اللَّهُ} الآيَةَ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ}
titles.english: Chapter: "And there is no sin on you if you make a hint of betrothal or conceal it in yourself..."

titles.arabic: باب النَّظَرِ إِلَى الْمَرْأَةِ قَبْلَ التَّزْوِيجِ
titles.english: Chapter: (It is permissible) to look at a woman before marrying.

titles.arabic: بَابُ مَنْ قَالَ لاَ نِكَاحَ إِلاَّ بِوَلِيٍّ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever said, "A marriage is not valid except through the Wali."

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا كَانَ الْوَلِيُّ هُوَ الْخَاطِبَ
titles.english: Chapter: If the guardian himself is the suitor

titles.arabic: باب إِنْكَاحِ الرَّجُلِ وَلَدَهُ الصِّغَارَ
titles.english: Chapter: Giving one's young children in marriage

titles.arabic: باب تَزْوِيجِ الأَبِ ابْنَتَهُ مِنَ الإِمَامِ
titles.english: Chapter: The marrying of a daughter by her father to a ruler

titles.arabic: بَابُ السُّلْطَانُ وَلِيٌّ بِقَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «زَوَّجْنَاكَهَا بِمَا مَعَكَ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ»
titles.english: Chapter: The ruler is regarded as a guardian

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يُنْكِحُ الأَبُ وَغَيْرُهُ الْبِكْرَ وَالثَّيِّبَ إِلاَّ بِرِضَاهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The father or the guardian cannot give a virgin or matron in marriage without her consent

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا زَوَّجَ ابْنَتَهُ وَهْىَ كَارِهَةٌ فَنِكَاحُهُ مَرْدُودٌ
titles.english: Chapter: If a man gives his daughter in marriage while she is averse to it, then such marriage is invalid

titles.arabic: باب تَزْوِيجِ الْيَتِيمَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The giving of an orphan girl in marriage

titles.arabic: بَابُ إِذَا قَالَ الْخَاطِبُ لِلْوَلِيِّ زَوِّجْنِي فُلاَنَةَ. فَقَالَ قَدْ زَوَّجْتُكَ بِكَذَا وَكَذَا. جَازَ النِّكَاحُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَقُلْ لِلزَّوْجِ أَرَضِيتَ أَوْ قَبِلْتَ
titles.english: Chapter: If the suitor says, "Marry me to so-and-so," and the guardian says, "I have married her to you for such Mahr"

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَخْطُبُ عَلَى خِطْبَةِ أَخِيهِ، حَتَّى يَنْكِحَ أَوْ يَدَعَ
titles.english: Chapter: None should ask for the hand of a lady who is already engaged to his brother (Muslim)

titles.arabic: باب تَفْسِيرِ تَرْكِ الْخِطْبَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The meaning of the cancelling of the engagement.

titles.arabic: باب الْخُطْبَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Khutba (for Nikah)

titles.arabic: باب ضَرْبِ الدُّفِّ فِي النِّكَاحِ وَالْوَلِيمَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Beating the tambourine during marriage ceremony and the wedding banquet.

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى ‏{‏وَآتُوا النِّسَاءَ صَدُقَاتِهِنَّ نِحْلَةً‏}‏
titles.english: Chapter: "And give to the women their Mahr with a good heart..."

titles.arabic: باب التَّزْوِيجِ عَلَى الْقُرْآنِ وَبِغَيْرِ صَدَاقٍ‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: To marry (a lady to) a man for what he knows of the Qur'an

titles.arabic: باب الْمَهْرِ بِالْعُرُوضِ وَخَاتَمٍ مِنْ حَدِيدٍ
titles.english: Chapter: To give Mahr in the form of material things

titles.arabic: باب الشُّرُوطِ فِي النِّكَاحِ‏
titles.english: Chapter: The conditions stipulated in marriage (contract)

titles.arabic: باب الشُّرُوطِ الَّتِي لاَ تَحِلُّ فِي النِّكَاحِ‏
titles.english: Chapter: Conditions not lawful in the marriage contract

titles.arabic: باب الصُّفْرَةِ لِلْمُتَزَوِّجِ
titles.english: Chapter: Yellow coloured perfume for a bridegroom

titles.arabic: بَابٌ
titles.english: Chapter

titles.arabic: باب كَيْفَ يُدْعَى لِلْمُتَزَوِّجِ
titles.english: Chapter: How to invoke good upon a person who has married recently?

titles.arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ لِلنِّسَاءِ اللاَّتِي يَهْدِينَ الْعَرُوسَ، وَلِلْعَرُوسِ
titles.english: Chapter: The invocation of those women who prepare the bride

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَحَبَّ الْبِنَاءَ قَبْلَ الْغَزْوِ
titles.english: Chapter: To consummate the marriage before going on a military campaign

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ بَنَى بِامْرَأَةٍ وَهْىَ بِنْتُ تِسْعِ سِنِينَ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever consummated his marriage with a lady of nine years of age

titles.arabic: باب الْبِنَاءِ فِي السَّفَرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Consummation of marriage during a journey

titles.arabic: باب الْبِنَاءِ بِالنَّهَارِ بِغَيْرِ مَرْكَبٍ وَلاَ نِيرَانٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Consummation of marriage during the daytime without a marriage procession or lighting of fires

titles.arabic: باب الأَنْمَاطِ وَنَحْوِهَا لِلنِّسَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: The curtains, beddings and similar thins designed for the women

titles.arabic: باب النِّسْوَةِ اللاَّتِي يَهْدِينَ الْمَرْأَةَ إِلَى زَوْجِهَا وَدُعَائِهِنَّ بِالْبَرَكَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The women who present the lady to her husband

titles.arabic: باب الْهَدِيَّةِ لِلْعَرُوسِ
titles.english: Chapter: The giving of a present to the bridegroom

titles.arabic: باب اسْتِعَارَةِ الثِّيَابِ لِلْعَرُوسِ وَغَيْرِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: To borrow the clothes, etc. for the bride

titles.arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا أَتَى أَهْلَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: What a man should say on having a sexual intercourse with his wife

titles.arabic: باب الْوَلِيمَةُ حَقٌّ
titles.english: Chapter: The Walima (wedding banquet) is obligatory

titles.arabic: باب الْوَلِيمَةِ وَلَوْ بِشَاةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Walima is to be given even with one sheep

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَوْلَمَ عَلَى بَعْضِ نِسَائِهِ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ بَعْضٍ
titles.english: Chapter: A bigger Walima on marrying some wives than the other wives

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَوْلَمَ بِأَقَلَّ مِنْ شَاةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Walima of less than one sheep

titles.arabic: باب حَقِّ إِجَابَةِ الْوَلِيمَةِ وَالدَّعْوَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To accept the invitation to a Walima

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ تَرَكَ الدَّعْوَةَ فَقَدْ عَصَى اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: If somebody refuses an invitation

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَجَابَ إِلَى كُرَاعٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever accepted the invitation to a meal of trotters

titles.arabic: باب إِجَابَةِ الدَّاعِي فِي الْعُرْسِ وَغَيْرِه
titles.english: Chapter: To accept the invitation to a party

titles.arabic: باب ذَهَابِ النِّسَاءِ وَالصِّبْيَانِ إِلَى الْعُرْسِ
titles.english: Chapter: The attendance of women and children at a wedding party

titles.arabic: باب هَلْ يَرْجِعُ إِذَا رَأَى مُنْكَرًا فِي الدَّعْوَةِ‏
titles.english: Chapter: Should a person return if he sees something objectionable in the party?

titles.arabic: باب قِيَامِ الْمَرْأَةِ عَلَى الرِّجَالِ فِي الْعُرْسِ وَخِدْمَتِهِمْ بِالنَّفْسِ
titles.english: Chapter: The serving of the bride herself for the men at (her) marriage party

titles.arabic: باب النَّقِيعِ وَالشَّرَابِ الَّذِي لاَ يُسْكِرُ فِي الْعُرْسِ
titles.english: Chapter: An-Naqi and other non-toxicant drinks at a wedding party

titles.arabic: باب الْمُدَارَاةِ مَعَ النِّسَاءِ‏
titles.english: Chapter: To be polite and kind to the women

titles.arabic: باب الْوَصَاةِ بِالنِّسَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: The exhortation of taking care of the women

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏قُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ وَأَهْلِيكُمْ نَارًا‏} ‏
titles.english: Chapter: "Ward off from yourself and your families a Fire whose fuel is men and stones..."

titles.arabic: باب حُسْنِ الْمُعَاشَرَةِ مَعَ الأَهْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: To treat the family in a polite and kind manner

titles.arabic: باب مَوْعِظَةِ الرَّجُلِ ابْنَتَهُ لِحَالِ زَوْجِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: Advice to a daughter regarding her husband

titles.arabic: باب صَوْمِ الْمَرْأَةِ بِإِذْنِ زَوْجِهَا تَطَوُّعًا
titles.english: Chapter: A woman should not observe Saum except husband's consent (is 'with her' missing here?)

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا بَاتَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ مُهَاجِرَةً فِرَاشَ زَوْجِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: If a woman deserts her husband's bed

titles.arabic: باب لاَ تَأْذَنُ الْمَرْأَةُ فِي بَيْتِ زَوْجِهَا لأَحَدٍ إِلاَّ بِإِذْنِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: A woman should not allow anyone to enter the house except husband's consent (here too)
Chapter

titles.arabic: باب كُفْرَانِ الْعَشِيرِ
titles.english: Chapter: To be unthankful to the husband

titles.arabic: باب لِزَوْجِكَ عَلَيْكَ حَقٌّ
titles.english: Chapter: Your wife has a right over you

titles.arabic: باب الْمَرْأَةُ رَاعِيَةٌ فِي بَيْتِ زَوْجِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The woman is a guardian in her husband's house

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {الرِّجَالُ قَوَّامُونَ عَلَى النِّسَاءِ بِمَا فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَى بَعْضٍ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيًّا كَبِيرًا}
titles.english: Chapter: "Men are protectors and maintainers of women."

titles.arabic: باب هِجْرَةِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم نِسَاءَهُ فِي غَيْرِ بُيُوتِهِنَّ
titles.english: Chapter: The decision of the Prophet (saws) not to share the beds with his wives and to stay away

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُكْرَهُ مِنْ ضَرْبِ النِّسَاءِ وَقَوْلِهِ ‏{‏وَاضْرِبُوهُنَّ‏}‏ ضَرْبًا غَيْرَ مُبَرِّحٍ‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: The beating of women is disapproved

titles.arabic: باب لاَ تُطِيعُ الْمَرْأَةُ زَوْجَهَا فِي مَعْصِيَةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Not to obey the husband if he orders to do something sinful

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَإِنِ امْرَأَةٌ خَافَتْ مِنْ بَعْلِهَا نُشُوزًا أَوْ إِعْرَاضًا}
titles.english: Chapter: "If a woman fears cruelty or desertion on her husband's part..."

titles.arabic: باب الْعَزْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: The coitus interruptus

titles.arabic: باب الْقُرْعَةِ بَيْنَ النِّسَاءِ إِذَا أَرَادَ سَفَرًا
titles.english: Chapter: To draw lots among the wives for a journey

titles.arabic: باب الْمَرْأَةِ تَهَبُ يَوْمَهَا مِنْ زَوْجِهَا لِضَرَّتِهَا وَكَيْفَ يُقْسِمُ ذَلِكَ
titles.english: Chapter: To give up the turn with the husband to one of his other wives

titles.arabic: بَابُ الْعَدْلِ بَيْنَ النِّسَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: To deal justly between the women (wives)

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ الْبِكْرَ عَلَى الثَّيِّبِ
titles.english: Chapter: Marrying a virgin while already having a matron wife

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ الثَّيِّبَ عَلَى الْبِكْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Marrying a matron while already having a virgin wife

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ طَافَ عَلَى نِسَائِهِ فِي غُسْلٍ وَاحِدٍ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever had sexual intercourse with all his wives and then took one bath only

titles.arabic: باب دُخُولِ الرَّجُلِ عَلَى نِسَائِهِ فِي الْيَوْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: If a man goes to all his wives in one day

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا اسْتَأْذَنَ الرَّجُلُ نِسَاءَهُ فِي أَنْ يُمَرَّضَ فِي بَيْتِ بَعْضِهِنَّ، فَأَذِنَّ لَهُ‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: If a man takes the permission of his wives so as to stay in the house of one of them

titles.arabic: باب حُبِّ الرَّجُلِ بَعْضَ نِسَائِهِ أَفْضَلَ مِنْ بَعْضٍ
titles.english: Chapter: To love some of the wives more than the others

titles.arabic: باب الْمُتَشَبِّعِ بِمَا لَمْ يَنَلْ، وَمَا يُنْهَى مِنِ افْتِخَارِ الضَّرَّةِ
titles.english: Chapter: To claim having more things or better qualities than one really has

titles.arabic: باب الْغَيْرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Ghaira (i.e. honour, prestige or self-respect)

titles.arabic: باب غَيْرَةِ النِّسَاءِ وَوَجْدِهِنَّ
titles.english: Chapter: The jealousy of women and their anger

titles.arabic: باب ذَبِّ الرَّجُلِ عَنِ ابْنَتِهِ، فِي الْغَيْرَةِ وَالإِنْصَافِ
titles.english: Chapter: Attempt to prevent the daughter's jealousy

titles.arabic: باب يَقِلُّ الرِّجَالُ وَيَكْثُرُ النِّسَاءُ
titles.english: Chapter: Men will decrease and women will increase

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَخْلُوَنَّ رَجُلٌ بِامْرَأَةٍ إِلاَّ ذُو مَحْرَمٍ، وَالدُّخُولُ عَلَى الْمُغِيبَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: A man should not stay with a woman in seclusion

titles.arabic: باب مَا يَجُوزُ أَنْ يَخْلُوَ الرَّجُلُ بِالْمَرْأَةِ عِنْدَ النَّاسِ
titles.english: Chapter: Private meeting between a man and a woman not secluded from the people

titles.arabic: باب مَا يُنْهَى مِنْ دُخُولِ الْمُتَشَبِّهِينَ بِالنِّسَاءِ عَلَى الْمَرْأَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Effeminate men should not enter upon women

titles.arabic: باب نَظَرِ الْمَرْأَةِ إِلَى الْحَبَشِ وَنَحْوِهِمْ مِنْ غَيْرِ رِيبَةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The looking of a woman at the Ethiopians and the like

titles.arabic: باب خُرُوجِ النِّسَاءِ لِحَوَائِجِهِنَّ
titles.english: Chapter: The going out of women for their needs

titles.arabic: باب اسْتِئْذَانِ الْمَرْأَةِ زَوْجَهَا فِي الْخُرُوجِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ وَغَيْرِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: The permission taken by a woman from her husband to go to the mosque

titles.arabic: باب مَا يَحِلُّ مِنَ الدُّخُولِ وَالنَّظَرِ إِلَى النِّسَاءِ فِي الرَّضَاعِ
titles.english: Chapter: Visiting or looking at women having foster suckling reltions

titles.arabic: باب لاَ تُبَاشِرُ الْمَرْأَةُ الْمَرْأَةَ فَتَنْعَتَهَا لِزَوْجِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: A woman should not look at or touch the body of another woman to describe to her husband

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ الرَّجُلِ لأَطُوفَنَّ اللَّيْلَةَ عَلَى نِسَائِي
titles.english: Chapter: "I will go round all my wives tonight."

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَطْرُقْ أَهْلَهُ لَيْلاً إِذَا أَطَالَ الْغَيْبَةَ مَخَافَةَ، أَنْ يُخَوِّنَهُمْ أَوْ يَلْتَمِسَ عَثَرَاتِهِمْ
titles.english: Chapter: If a man is away for a long time, he should not enter his house at night.

titles.arabic: باب طَلَبِ الْوَلَدِ
titles.english: Chapter: Seeking to beget children

titles.arabic: باب تَسْتَحِدُّ الْمُغِيبَةُ وَتَمْتَشِطُ ‏{‏الشَّعِثَةُ‏}‏
titles.english: Chapter: The woman should shave her pubic hair, and should comb the hair.

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَلاَ يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلاَّ لِبُعُولَتِهِنَّ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {لَمْ يَظْهَرُوا عَلَى عَوْرَاتِ النِّسَاءِ}
titles.english: Chapter: "And not to reveal their adornments except to their husbands,..."

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَالَّذِينَ لَمْ يَبْلُغُوا الْحُلُمَ مِنْكُمْ‏} ‏
titles.english: Chapter: 'And those among you who have not come to the age of puberty."

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ الرَّجُلِ لِصَاحِبِهِ هَلْ أَعْرَسْتُمُ اللَّيْلَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: The man's poking his daughter in the flank while admonishing her

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّسَاءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحْصُوا الْعِدَّةَ}
titles.english: Chapter: "O Prophet! When you divorce women, divorce them at their 'Idda and count their 'Idda."

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا طُلِّقَتِ الْحَائِضُ يُعْتَدُّ بِذَلِكَ الطَّلاَقِ
titles.english: Chapter: Divorce during the menses is counted as one legal divorce.

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ طَلَّقَ وَهَلْ يُوَاجِهُ الرَّجُلُ امْرَأَتَهُ بِالطَّلاَقِ
titles.english: Chapter: Should a man tell his wife face to face that she is divorced

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَجَازَ طَلاَقَ الثَّلاَثِ
titles.english: Chapter: To divorce one's wife thrice (at a time)

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ خَيَّرَ نِسَاءَهُ
titles.english: Chapter: Giving option to the wives.

titles.arabic: بَابُ إِذَا قَالَ فَارَقْتُكِ أَوْ سَرَّحْتُكِ
titles.english: Chapter: If a man says (to his wife): "I have parted with you," or "I have released you"

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ قَالَ لاِمْرَأَتِهِ أَنْتِ عَلَىَّ حَرَامٌ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever said to his wife: "You are Haram for me."

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ لَكَ‏} ‏
titles.english: Chapter: "O Prophet! Why do you forbid that which Allah has allowed to you...?

titles.arabic: بَابُ لاَ طَلاَقَ قَبْلَ النِّكَاحِ
titles.english: Chapter: There is no divorce before marriage

titles.arabic: بَابُ إِذَا قَالَ لاِمْرَأَتِهِ وَهْوَ مُكْرَهٌ هَذِهِ أُخْتِي. فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ
titles.english: Chapter: If, under compulsion somebody says about his wife, "She is my sister."

titles.arabic: باب الطَّلاَقِ فِي الإِغْلاَقِ
titles.english: Chapter: A divorce given in a state of anger, under compulsion or under the effect of intoxicants or insanity

titles.arabic: باب الْخُلْعِ وَكَيْفَ الطَّلاَقُ فِيهِ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Khul' and how a divorce is given according to it

titles.arabic: باب الشِّقَاقِ
titles.english: Chapter: Ash-Shiqaq (the breach between the man and his wife).

titles.arabic: باب لاَ يَكُونُ بَيْعُ الأَمَةِ طَلاَقًا
titles.english: Chapter: Selling a female slave does not lead to her divorce.

titles.arabic: باب خِيَارِ الأَمَةِ تَحْتَ الْعَبْدِ
titles.english: Chapter: A female slave, whose husband is a slave, has the option to keep him or leave him (when she is manumitted).

titles.arabic: باب شَفَاعَةِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي زَوْجِ بَرِيرَةَ
titles.english: Chapter: The intercession of the Prophet (saws) for Barira's husband.
Chapter

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَلاَ تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكَاتِ حَتَّى يُؤْمِنَّ وَلأَمَةٌ مُؤْمِنَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِنْ مُشْرِكَةٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ}
titles.english: Chapter: "Do not marry Al-Mushirkat till they believe..."

titles.arabic: باب نِكَاحِ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكَاتِ وَعِدَّتِهِنَّ
titles.english: Chapter: Marrying Al-Mushrikat who had embraced Islam; and their 'Idda.

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا أَسْلَمَتِ الْمُشْرِكَةُ أَوِ النَّصْرَانِيَّةُ تَحْتَ الذِّمِّيِّ أَوِ الْحَرْبِيِّ‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: If an idolatress or a Christian woman becomes a Muslim while she is the wife of Dhimmi or a Mushrik at war with the Muslims?

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {لِلَّذِينَ يُؤْلُونَ مِنْ نِسَائِهِمْ تَرَبُّصُ أَرْبَعَةِ أَشْهُرٍ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ} {فَإِنْ فَاءُوا} رَجَعُوا
titles.english: Chapter: "Those who take an oath, not to have sexual relations with their wives, must wait four months."

titles.arabic: باب حُكْمِ الْمَفْقُودِ فِي أَهْلِهِ وَمَالِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: The regulations concerning the property and family of a lost person

titles.arabic: بَابُ الظِّهَارِ
titles.english: Chapter: Az-Zihar

titles.arabic: باب الإِشَارَةِ فِي الطَّلاَقِ وَالأُمُورِ
titles.english: Chapter: Using gestures to express the decision of divorce

titles.arabic: باب اللِّعَانِ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Li'an

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا عَرَّضَ بِنَفْىِ الْوَلَدِ
titles.english: Chapter: If a husband suspects his paternity to a child.

titles.arabic: باب إِحْلاَفِ الْمُلاَعِنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Commanding those who are involved in a case of Li'an to take the oath.

titles.arabic: باب يَبْدَأُ الرَّجُلُ بِالتَّلاَعُنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The man should start the process of Li'an.

titles.arabic: باب اللِّعَانِ وَمَنْ طَلَّقَ بَعْدَ اللِّعَانِ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Li'an, and divorce after the process of Li'an.

titles.arabic: باب التَّلاَعُنِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ
titles.english: Chapter: To carry out Li'an in a Mosque.

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ لَوْ كُنْتُ رَاجِمًا بِغَيْرِ بَيِّنَةٍ ‏"‏
titles.english: Chapter: "If I were to stone any person to death without witnesses."

titles.arabic: باب صَدَاقِ الْمُلاَعَنَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The Mahr in the case Li'an

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ الإِمَامِ لِلْمُتَلاَعِنَيْنِ إِنَّ أَحَدَكُمَا كَاذِبٌ فَهَلْ مِنْكُمَا تَائِبٌ
titles.english: Chapter: "Surely one of you two is a liar; so will one of you repent (to Allah)?"

titles.arabic: باب التَّفْرِيقِ بَيْنَ الْمُتَلاَعِنَيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: The separation between those who are involved in a case of Li'an.

titles.arabic: باب يَلْحَقُ الْوَلَدُ بِالْمُلاَعِنَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The child is to be given to the lady (accused by her husband).

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ الإِمَامِ اللَّهُمَّ بَيِّنْ
titles.english: Chapter: "O Allah! Reveal the truth."

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا طَلَّقَهَا ثَلاَثًا ثُمَّ تَزَوَّجَتْ بَعْدَ الْعِدَّةِ زَوْجًا غَيْرَهُ فَلَمْ يَمَسَّهَا
titles.english: Chapter: Marriage of a divorced woman to another man but he does not consummate his marriage with her

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَاللاَّئِي يَئِسْنَ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ مِنْ نِسَائِكُمْ إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ}
titles.english: Chapter: "And those of your women as have passed the age of monthly courses..."

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَأُولاَتُ الأَحْمَالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَنْ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَّ}
titles.english: Chapter: "For those who are pregnant, their 'Idda is until they laydown their burdens."

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَالْمُطَلَّقَاتُ يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنْفُسِهِنَّ ثَلاَثَةَ قُرُوءٍ}
titles.english: Chapter: "And divorced women shall wait for three menstrual periods."

titles.arabic: باب قِصَّةِ فَاطِمَةَ بِنْتِ قَيْسٍ
titles.english: Chapter: The story of Fatima bint Qais

titles.arabic: باب الْمُطَلَّقَةِ إِذَا خُشِيَ عَلَيْهَا فِي مَسْكَنِ زَوْجِهَا أَنْ يُقْتَحَمَ عَلَيْهَا، أَوْ تَبْذُوَ عَلَى أَهْلِهَا بِفَاحِشَةٍ
titles.english: Chapter: If a divorced lady is afraid that she may be attacked in her husband's house.

titles.arabic: بَابُ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {وَلاَ يَحِلُّ لَهُنَّ أَنْ يَكْتُمْنَ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ فِي أَرْحَامِهِنَّ} مِنَ الْحَيْضِ وَالْحَبَلِ
titles.english: Chapter: "And it is not lawful for them to conceal what Allah has created in their wombs."

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَبُعُولَتُهُنَّ أَحَقُّ بِرَدِّهِنَّ}
titles.english: Chapter: "And their husbands have the better right to take them back..."

titles.arabic: باب مُرَاجَعَةِ الْحَائِضِ
titles.english: Chapter: To take back one's wife while in her menses.

titles.arabic: باب تُحِدُّ الْمُتَوَفَّى عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَعَشْرًا
titles.english: Chapter: A widow should mourn for four months and ten days.

titles.arabic: باب الْكُحْلِ لِلْحَادَّةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Can a mourning lady use kohl?

titles.arabic: باب الْقُسْطِ لِلْحَادَّةِ عِنْدَ الطُّهْرِ
titles.english: Chapter: Qust (incense) may be used by a mourning after being cleaned from her menses.

titles.arabic: باب تَلْبَسُ الْحَادَّةُ ثِيَابَ الْعَصْبِ
titles.english: Chapter: A mourning lady can wear clothes of 'Asb.

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {وَالَّذِينَ يُتَوَفَّوْنَ مِنْكُمْ وَيَذَرُونَ أَزْوَاجًا} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ}
titles.english: Chapter: "And those of you who die and leave behind wives..."

titles.arabic: باب مَهْرِ الْبَغِيِّ وَالنِّكَاحِ الْفَاسِدِ
titles.english: Chapter: The earnings of a prostitute and the illegal wedding.

titles.arabic: باب الْمَهْرِ لِلْمَدْخُولِ عَلَيْهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The Mahr of the lady whose husband entered upon her to consummate his marriage.

titles.arabic: باب الْمُتْعَةِ لِلَّتِي لَمْ يُفْرَضْ لَهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The gift given by a husband to a divorced lady for whom Mahr has not been fixed.

titles.arabic: باب فَضْلِ النَّفَقَةِ عَلَى الأَهْلِ
titles.english: Chapter: The superiority of providing for one's family

titles.arabic: باب وُجُوبِ النَّفَقَةِ عَلَى الأَهْلِ وَالْعِيَالِ
titles.english: Chapter: It is obligatory to spend for one's wife and household.

titles.arabic: باب حَبْسِ نَفَقَةِ الرَّجُلِ قُوتَ سَنَةٍ عَلَى أَهْلِهِ، وَكَيْفَ نَفَقَاتُ الْعِيَالِ
titles.english: Chapter: To provide one's family with food sufficient for one year in advance

titles.arabic: باب نَفَقَةِ الْمَرْأَةِ إِذَا غَابَ عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا وَنَفَقَةِ الْوَلَدِ
titles.english: Chapter: The expenditure of a woman and her child, whose husband is away from her

titles.arabic: بَابٌ
titles.english: Chapter: "The mothers shall give suck to their children for two whole years, for those who desire to complete the term of suckling..."

titles.arabic: باب عَمَلِ الْمَرْأَةِ فِي بَيْتِ زَوْجِهَا
titles.english: Chapter: The working of a lady in her husband's house.

titles.arabic: باب خَادِمِ الْمَرْأَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: A servant for one's wife

titles.arabic: باب خِدْمَةِ الرَّجُلِ فِي أَهْلِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: A man's serving his family.

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يُنْفِقِ الرَّجُلُ فَلِلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ تَأْخُذَ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمِهِ مَا يَكْفِيهَا وَوَلَدَهَا بِالْمَعْرُوفِ
titles.english: Chapter: If a man does not provide for his family

titles.arabic: باب حِفْظِ الْمَرْأَةِ زَوْجَهَا فِي ذَاتِ يَدِهِ وَالنَّفَقَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: A woman should take care of the wealth of her husband.

titles.arabic: باب كِسْوَةِ الْمَرْأَةِ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ
titles.english: Chapter: Providing one's wife with clothes reasonably

titles.arabic: باب عَوْنِ الْمَرْأَةِ زَوْجَهَا فِي وَلَدِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: A lady should help her husband in looking after his children

titles.arabic: باب نَفَقَةِ الْمُعْسِرِ عَلَى أَهْلِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: The expenditure of a poor man on his family

titles.arabic: باب ‏ {‏وَعَلَى الْوَارِثِ مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ‏} ‏ ، وَهَلْ عَلَى الْمَرْأَةِ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ؟
titles.english: Chapter: "And on the heir is incumbent the like of that..."

titles.arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ مَنْ تَرَكَ كَلاًّ أَوْ ضَيَاعًا فَإِلَىَّ ‏"‏‏.‏
titles.english: Chapter: "If one dies leaving debts to be repaid or dependents to be taken care of, it is for me."

titles.arabic: باب الْمَرَاضِعِ مِنَ الْمَوَالِيَاتِ وَغَيْرِهِنَّ
titles.english: Chapter: Freed female slaves or any other women can be wet nurses.
Chapter

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدْ مَاءً وَلاَ تُرَابًا
titles.english: Chapter: What to do if neither water nor earth is available

titles.arabic: باب التَّيَمُّمِ فِي الْحَضَرِ، إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدِ الْمَاءَ، وَخَافَ فَوْتَ الصَّلاَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: The performance of Tayammum by a non-traveller (is permissible) when water is not available and when one is afraid that the time of Salat (prayer) may elapse

titles.arabic: باب الْمُتَيَمِّمُ هَلْ يَنْفُخُ فِيهِمَا
titles.english: Chapter: Can a person blow off the dust from his hands in performing Tayammum (before passing them over his face)

titles.arabic: باب التَّيَمُّمُ لِلْوَجْهِ وَالْكَفَّيْنِ
titles.english: Chapter: Tayammum is for the hands and the face

titles.arabic: باب الصَّعِيدُ الطَّيِّبُ وَضُوءُ الْمُسْلِمِ ، يَكْفِيهِ مِنَ الْمَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: Clean earth is sufficient for a Muslim as a substitute for water for ablution (if he does not find water)

titles.arabic: باب إِذَا خَافَ الْجُنُبُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ الْمَرَضَ أَوِ الْمَوْتَ أَوْ خَافَ الْعَطَشَ، تَيَمَّمَ
titles.english: Chapter: A Junub can perform Tayammum if he is afraid of disease, death or thirst

titles.arabic: باب التَّيَمُّمُ ضَرْبَةٌ
titles.english: Chapter: Tayammum with one light stroke (on the earth)
Chapter

titles.arabic: بَابُ وَقَوْلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى: {كُلُوا مِنْ طَيِّبَاتِ مَا رَزَقْنَاكُمْ}
titles.english: Chapter: Eat of the lawful things that We have provided you with..."

titles.arabic: باب التَّسْمِيَةِ عَلَى الطَّعَامِ وَالأَكْلِ بِالْيَمِينِ
titles.english: Chapter: Mention the Name of Allah on starting to eat, and eat with the right hand.

titles.arabic: باب الأَكْلِ مِمَّا يَلِيهِ
titles.english: Chapter: To eat of the dish what is nearer to you

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ تَتَبَّعَ حَوَالَىِ الْقَصْعَةِ مَعَ صَاحِبِهِ، إِذَا لَمْ يَعْرِفْ مِنْهُ كَرَاهِيَةً
titles.english: Chapter: Eating from around the dish while taking meal with someone else.

titles.arabic: باب التَّيَمُّنِ فِي الأَكْلِ وَغَيْرِهِ
titles.english: Chapter: To eat with right hand, and to start with the right side in doing other things.

titles.arabic: باب مَنْ أَكَلَ حَتَّى شَبِعَ
titles.english: Chapter: Whoever ate till he was satisfied

titles.arabic: بَابُ: {لَيْسَ عَلَى الأَعْمَى حَرَجٌ} إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: {لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ}
titles.english: Chapter: "There is no restriction on the blind..."

titles.arabic: باب الْخُبْزِ الْمُرَقَّقِ وَالأَكْلِ عَلَى الْخِوَانِ وَالسُّفْرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Thin bread and eating at a dining table.

titles.arabic: باب السَّوِيقِ
titles.english: Chapter: As-Sawiq

titles.arabic: باب مَا كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم لاَ يَأْكُلُ حَتَّى يُسَمَّى لَهُ فَيَعْلَمُ مَا هُوَ
titles.english: Chapter: The Prophet (saws) never used to eat anything unless it was named for him so that he might know what it was

titles.arabic: باب طَعَامُ الْوَاحِدِ يَكْفِي الاِثْنَيْنِ‏
titles.english: Chapter: The food of one person is sufficient for two persons.

titles.arabic: باب الْمُؤْمِنُ يَأْكُلُ فِي مِعًى وَاحِدٍ‏
titles.english: Chapter: A believer eats in one intestine

titles.arabic: باب الأَكْلِ مُتَّكِئًا‏
titles.english: Chapter: To eat while leaning

titles.arabic: بَابُ الشِّوَاءِ
titles.english: Chapter: Roasted (meat).

titles.arabic: باب الْخَزِيرَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Khazira (dish prepared with while flour and fat)

titles.arabic: باب الأَقِطِ
titles.english: Chapter: Al-Aqit (dried yoghourt)

titles.arabic: باب السِّلْقِ وَالشَّعِيرِ
titles.english: Chapter: As-Salq (a kind of beet) and barley

titles.arabic: باب النَّهْسِ وَانْتِشَالِ اللَّحْمِ
titles.english: Chapter: To seize and catch flesh with the teeth (while eating).

titles.arabic: باب تَعَرُّقِ الْعَضُدِ
titles.english: Chapter: To eat the flesh of a foreleg (by stripping the bone of its meat with the teeth).

titles.arabic: باب قَطْعِ اللَّحْمِ بِالسِّكِّينِ
titles.english: Chapter: To cut the meat with a knife

titles.arabic: باب مَا عَابَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم طَعَامًا
titles.english: Chapter: The Prophet (saws) never criticized any food.

titles.arabic: باب النَّفْخِ فِي الشَّعِيرِ
titles.english: Chapter: To blow barley (to remove the husk).

titles.arabic: باب مَا كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم وَأَصْحَابُهُ يَأْكُلُونَ
titles.english: Chapter: What the Prophet (saws) and his Companions used to eat.

titles.arabic: باب التَّلْبِينَةِ
titles.english: Chapter: At-Talbina (a kind of dish prepared from flour or bran)

titles.arabic: باب الثَّرِيدِ
titles.english: Chapter: Ath-Tharid (a dish prepared from meat and bread).

titles.arabic: باب شَاةٍ مَسْمُوطَةٍ وَالْكَتِفِ وَالْجَنْبِ
titles.english: Chapter: A roasted sheep.
